t_id.kemenag: Dan Al Quran ini adalah suatu kitab (peringatan) yang mempunyai berkah yang telah Kami turunkan. Maka mengapakah kamu mengingkarinya?
t_id.sabiq: Al-Qur'ân ini adalah peringatan yang banyak mengandung kebaikan, yang Kami turunkan kepada kalian seperti halnya Kami menurunkan peringatan kepada Mûsâ. Bagaimana kalian mengingkarinya, padahal kalian adalah orang yang paling pantas untuk mengimaninya?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ini) yakni Alquran ini (adalah suatu kitab peringatan yang mempunyai berkah yang telah Kami turunkan. Maka mengapa kalian mengingkarinya?) Istifham atau kata tanya di sini mengandung pengertian mencemoohkan.
t_id.iwq: Dan Al Al-Quran ini adalah suatu kitab (peringatan) yang mempunyai berkah yang telah Kami turunkan. Maka mengapakah kamu mengingkarinya?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وهذا» أي القرآن «ذكر مبارك أنزلناه أفأنتم له منكرون» الاستفهام فيه للتوبيخ
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَهَٰذَا ذِكۡرٞ مُّبَارَكٌ أَنزَلۡنَٰهُۚ أَفَأَنتُمۡ لَهُۥ مُنكِرُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ini) yakni Alquran ini (adalah suatu kitab peringatan yang mempunyai berkah yang telah Kami turunkan. Maka mengapa kalian mengingkarinya?) Istifham atau kata tanya di sini mengandung pengertian mencemoohkan

ar: ۞ وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَیۡنَاۤ إِبۡرَ ٰهِیمَ رُشۡدَهُۥ مِن قَبۡلُ وَكُنَّا بِهِۦ عَـٰلِمِینَ
ar_simple: ۞ وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ رُشْدَهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَكُنَّا بِهِ عَالِمِينَ

t.sahih-int: And We had certainly given Abraham his sound judgement before, and We were of him well-Knowing
t.asad: AND, INDEED, long before [the time of Moses] We vouchsafed unto Abraham his consciousness of what is right; and We were aware of [what moved] him
t.yusuf-ali: We bestowed aforetime on Abraham his rectitude of conduct, and well were We acquainted with him
t.pickthall: And We verily gave Abraham of old his proper course, and We were Aware of him
t.arberry: We gave Abraham aforetime his rectitude -- for We knew him
t.hilali-khan: And indeed We bestowed aforetime on Ibrahim (Abraham) his (portion of) guidance, and We were Well-Acquainted with him (as to his Belief in the Oneness of Allah, etc)
t.itani: We gave Abraham his integrity formerly, and We knew him well.
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed We bestowed aforetime on Ibrahim his guidance, and We were Well-Acquainted with him.
t.qarib: Before [Moses] We gave Abraham sound judgment and We knew him well
t.wahiduddin: Before this We gave Abraham his guidance. We knew him well
t.shakir: And certainly We gave to Ibrahim his rectitude before, and We knew him fully well
t.haleem: Long ago We bestowed right judgement on Abraham and We knew him well
t.maariful: Even earlier We had given Ibrāhīm his right course, and We knew him well
t.khattab: And indeed, We had granted Abraham sound judgment early on, for We knew him well ˹to be worthy of it˺
t.ghali: And indeed We already brought Ibrahim (Abrahem) earlier his right-mindedness and We were Knowledgeable of him
t.irving: We gave Abraham his integrity long ago, and We were (fully) Conscious of him
t.iwq: And We had certainly given Abraham his sound judgement before, and We were of him well-Knowing

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami anugerahkan kepada Ibrahim hidayah kebenaran sebelum (Musa dan Harun), dan adalah Kami mengetahui (keadaan)nya.
t_id.sabiq: Sungguh, Ibrâhîm telah Kami berikan kematangan dan daya pikir dalam mencari kebenaran dengan tulus, sebelum Mûsâ dan Hârûn. Dan Kami Maha Mengetahui tentang kondisi dan keutamaannya hingga pantas membawa pesan-pesan Tuhan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami anugerahkan kepada Ibrahim hidayah kebenaran sebelumnya) sebelum ia mencapai umur balig (dan adalah Kami mengetahuinya) bahwa dia layak untuk menerima hal tersebut.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami anugerahkan kepada Ibrahim hidayah kebenaran sebelum (Musa dan Harun), dan adalah Kami mengetahui (keadaan)nya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد آتينا إبراهيم رشده من قبل» أي هداه قبل بلوغه «وكنا به عالمين» بأنه أهل لذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَآ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ رُشۡدَهُۥ مِن قَبۡلُ وَكُنَّا بِهِۦ عَٰلِمِينَ (رُشْدَهُ: هُدَاهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya telah Kami anugerahkan kepada Ibrahim hidayah kebenaran sebelumnya) sebelum ia mencapai umur balig (dan adalah Kami mengetahuinya) bahwa dia layak untuk menerima hal tersebut

ar: إِذۡ قَالَ لِأَبِیهِ وَقَوۡمِهِۦ مَا هَـٰذِهِ ٱلتَّمَاثِیلُ ٱلَّتِیۤ أَنتُمۡ لَهَا عَـٰكِفُونَ
ar_simple: إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِ مَا هَٰذِهِ التَّمَاثِيلُ الَّتِي أَنْتُمْ لَهَا عَاكِفُونَ

t.sahih-int: When he said to his father and his people, "What are these statues to which you are devoted
t.asad: when he said unto his father and his people, “What are these images to which you are so intensely devoted?”
t.yusuf-ali: Behold! he said to his father and his people, "What are these images, to which ye are (so assiduously) devoted
t.pickthall: When he said unto his father and his folk: What are these images unto which ye pay devotion
t.arberry: when he said to his father and his people, 'What are these statues unto which you are cleaving
t.hilali-khan: When he said to his father and his people: "What are these images, to which you are devoted
t.itani: When he said to his father and his people, “What are these statues to which you are devoted?”
t.mubarakpuri: When he said to his father and his people: "What are these images to which you are devoted"
t.qarib: when he said to his father and his people, "What are these statues to which you are devoted
t.wahiduddin: When he asked his father and his people, "What are these images to which you are so devoted
t.shakir: When he said to his father and his people: What are these images to whose worship you cleave
t.haleem: He said to his father and his people, ‘What are these images to which you are so devoted?’
t.maariful: when he said to his father and his people, “What are these statues you are devoted to?”
t.khattab: ˹Remember˺ when he questioned his father and his people, “What are these statues to which you are so devoted?”
t.ghali: As he said to his father and his people, "What are these statues to which you are consecrating (yourselves)
t.irving: when he told his father and his folk: "What are these images which you are so devoted to
t.iwq: When he said to his father and his people, "What are these statues to which you are devoted

t_id.kemenag: (Ingatlah), ketika Ibrahim berkata kepada bapaknya dan kaumnya: "Patung-patung apakah ini yang kamu tekun beribadat kepadanya?"
t_id.sabiq: Ingatlah, wahai Rasulullah, ketika Ibrâhîm berkata kepada ayahnya dan pengikutnya seraya meremehkan patung-patung yang dahulu mereka agungkan dan mereka sembah, "Apa itu patung-patung yang kalian sembah?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu ketika Ibrahim berkata kepada bapaknya dan kaumnya, "Patung-patung apakah ini) maksudnya, apa kegunaan berhala-berhala ini (yang kalian tekun beribadah kepadanya?") kalian dengan tekun menyembahnya.
t_id.iwq: (Ingatlah), ketika Ibrahim berkata kepada bapaknya dan kaumnya: "Patung-patung apakah ini yang kamu tekun beribadat kepadanya?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إذ قال لأبيه وقومه ما هذه التماثيل» الأصنام «التي أنتم لها عاكفون» أي على عبادتها مقيمون
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِذۡ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوۡمِهِۦ مَا هَٰذِهِ ٱلتَّمَاثِيلُ ٱلَّتِيٓ أَنتُمۡ لَهَا عَٰكِفُونَ (التَّمَاثِيلُ: الأَصْنَامُ الَّتِي صَنَعْتُمُوهَا, عَاكِفُونَ: مُقِيمُونَ عَلَى عِبَادَتِهَا، مُلَازِمُونَ لَهَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu ketika Ibrahim berkata kepada bapaknya dan kaumnya, "Patung-patung apakah ini) maksudnya, apa kegunaan berhala-berhala ini (yang kalian tekun beribadah kepadanya?") kalian dengan tekun menyembahnya

ar: قَالُوا۟ وَجَدۡنَاۤ ءَابَاۤءَنَا لَهَا عَـٰبِدِینَ
ar_simple: قَالُوا وَجَدْنَا آبَاءَنَا لَهَا عَابِدِينَ

t.sahih-int: They said, "We found our fathers worshippers of them
t.asad: They answered: “We found our forefathers worshipping them.”
t.yusuf-ali: They said, "We found our fathers worshipping them
t.pickthall: They said: We found our fathers worshippers of them
t.arberry: They said, 'We found our fathers serving them
t.hilali-khan: They said:"We found our fathers worshipping them
t.itani: They said, “We found our parents worshiping them.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "We found our fathers worshipping them."
t.qarib: They said, "We found our fathers worshipping them
t.wahiduddin: They replied, "We found our fathers worshipping them
t.shakir: They said: We found our fathers worshipping them
t.haleem: They replied, ‘We found our fathers worshipping them.’
t.maariful: They said, “We found our fathers worshipping them,”
t.khattab: They replied, “We found our forefathers worshipping them.”
t.ghali: They said, "We found our fathers worshiping them
t.irving: They said: "We found our forefathers serving them
t.iwq: And they said, "We found our fathers worshippers of them

t_id.kemenag: Mereka menjawab: "Kami mendapati bapak-bapak kami menyembahnya".
t_id.sabiq: Mereka menjawab, "Kami melihat nenek moyang kami mengagungkan dan menyembahnya, maka kami pun mengikuti jejak mereka."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka menjawab, "Kami mendapatkan bapak-bapak kami menyembahnya") maka kami mengikuti mereka.
t_id.iwq: Mereka menjawab: "Kami mendapati bapak-bapak kami menyembahnya".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا وجدنا آباءنا لها عابدين» فاقتدنيا بهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ وَجَدۡنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا لَهَا عَٰبِدِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka menjawab, "Kami mendapatkan bapak-bapak kami menyembahnya") maka kami mengikuti mereka

ar: قَالَ لَقَدۡ كُنتُمۡ أَنتُمۡ وَءَابَاۤؤُكُمۡ فِی ضَلَـٰلࣲ مُّبِینࣲ
ar_simple: قَالَ لَقَدْ كُنْتُمْ أَنْتُمْ وَآبَاؤُكُمْ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُبِينٍ

t.sahih-int: He said, "You were certainly, you and your fathers, in manifest error
t.asad: Said he: “Indeed, you and your forefathers have obviously gone astray!”
t.yusuf-ali: He said, "Indeed ye have been in manifest error - ye and your fathers
t.pickthall: He said: Verily ye and your fathers were in plain error
t.arberry: He said, 'Then assuredly you and your fathers have been in manifest error
t.hilali-khan: He said: "Indeed you and your fathers have been in manifest error
t.itani: He said, “You and your parents are in evident error.”
t.mubarakpuri: He said: "Indeed you and your fathers have been in manifest error."
t.qarib: He said, "You and your fathers have obviously gone astray
t.wahiduddin: Abraham said, "Indeed, you and your fathers have been clearly misguided
t.shakir: He said: Certainly you have been, (both) you and your fathers, in manifest error
t.haleem: He said, ‘You and your fathers have clearly gone astray.’
t.maariful: He said, “Surely you and your fathers have been in open error.”
t.khattab: He responded, “Indeed, you and your forefathers have been clearly astray.”
t.ghali: He said, "Indeed you were already, you and your fathers, in evident error
t.irving: He said: "You and your forefathers have been in such obvious error
t.iwq: And he said, "You were certainly, you and your fathers, in manifest error

t_id.kemenag: Ibrahim berkata: "Sesungguhnya kamu dan bapak-bapakmu berada dalam kesesatan yang nyata".
t_id.sabiq: Ibrâhîm berkata, "Kalian dan nenek moyang kalian dahulu benar-benar amat jauh dari kebenaran."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim berkata) kepada mereka, ("Sesungguhnya kalian dan bapak-bapak kalian) disebabkan menyembah berhala-berhala itu (berada dalam kesesatan yang nyata") yakni jelas sesatnya.
t_id.iwq: Ibrahim berkata: "Sesungguhnya kamu dan bapak-bapakmu berada dalam kesesatan yang nyata".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» لهم «لقد كنتم أنتم وآباؤكم» بعبادتها «في ضلال مبين» بَيّن
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ لَقَدۡ كُنتُمۡ أَنتُمۡ وَءَابَآؤُكُمۡ فِي ضَلَٰلٖ مُّبِينٖ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim berkata) kepada mereka, ("Sesungguhnya kalian dan bapak-bapak kalian) disebabkan menyembah berhala-berhala itu (berada dalam kesesatan yang nyata") yakni jelas sesatnya

ar: قَالُوۤا۟ أَجِئۡتَنَا بِٱلۡحَقِّ أَمۡ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱللَّـٰعِبِینَ
ar_simple: قَالُوا أَجِئْتَنَا بِالْحَقِّ أَمْ أَنْتَ مِنَ اللَّاعِبِينَ

t.sahih-int: They said, "Have you come to us with truth, or are you of those who jest
t.asad: They asked: “Hast thou come unto us [with this claim] in all earnest - or art thou one of those jesters?”
t.yusuf-ali: They said, "Have you brought us the Truth, or are you one of those who jest
t.pickthall: They said: Bringest thou unto us the truth, or art thou some jester
t.arberry: They said, 'What, hast thou come to us with the truth, or art thou one of those that play
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Have you brought us the truth, or are you one of those who play about
t.itani: They said, “Are you telling us the truth, or are you just playing?”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Have you brought us the Truth, or are you one of those who play about"
t.qarib: They said, "Have you come to us with the Truth, or are you playing games
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Have you brought us the truth or are you jesting
t.shakir: They said: Have you brought to us the truth, or are you one of the triflers
t.haleem: They asked, ‘Have you brought us the truth or are you just playing about?’
t.maariful: They said, “Did you come to us with truth or are you one of the triflers?”
t.khattab: They asked, “Have you come to us with the truth, or is this a joke?”
t.ghali: They said, "Have you come to us with the Truth, or are you of the ones that play
t.irving: They said: "Have you brought us the Truth, or are you just a trifler
t.iwq: And they said, "Have you come to us with truth, or are you of those who jest

t_id.kemenag: Mereka menjawab: "Apakah kamu datang kepada kami dengan sungguh-sungguh ataukah kamu termasuk orang-orang yang bermain-main?"
t_id.sabiq: Mereka menjawab, "Apakah kamu, dengan ucapanmu itu, membawa sesuatu yang kamu anggap benar kepada kami, atau hanya bermain-main saja dan tidak perlu diikuti?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka menjawab, "Apakah kamu datang kepada kami dengan sungguh-sungguh) apakah perkataanmu yang demikian itu sungguh-sungguh (atau apakah kamu termasuk orang yang bermain-main?") di dalam perkataanmu itu.
t_id.iwq: Mereka menjawab: "Apakah kamu datang kepada kami dengan sungguh-sungguh ataukah kamu termasuk orang-orang yang bermain-main?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا أجئتنا بالحق» في قولك هذا «أم أنت من اللاعبين» فيه
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُوٓاْ أَجِئۡتَنَا بِٱلۡحَقِّ أَمۡ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱللَّـٰعِبِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka menjawab, "Apakah kamu datang kepada kami dengan sungguh-sungguh) apakah perkataanmu yang demikian itu sungguh-sungguh (atau apakah kamu termasuk orang yang bermain-main?") di dalam perkataanmu itu

ar: قَالَ بَل رَّبُّكُمۡ رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّذِی فَطَرَهُنَّ وَأَنَا۠ عَلَىٰ ذَ ٰلِكُم مِّنَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِینَ
ar_simple: قَالَ بَلْ رَبُّكُمْ رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ الَّذِي فَطَرَهُنَّ وَأَنَا عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكُمْ مِنَ الشَّاهِدِينَ

t.sahih-int: He said, "[No], rather, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth who created them, and I, to that, am of those who testify
t.asad: He answered: “Nay, but your [true] Sustainer is the Sustainer of the heavens and the earth - He who has brought them into being: and I am one of those who bear witness to this [truth]!”
t.yusuf-ali: He said, "Nay, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, He Who created them (from nothing): and I am a witness to this (Truth)
t.pickthall: He said: Nay, but your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them; and I am of those who testify unto that
t.arberry: He said, 'Nay, but your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth who originated them, and I am one of those that bear witness thereunto
t.hilali-khan: He said: "Nay, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them and of that I am one of the witnesses
t.itani: He said, “Your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the One who created them, and I bear witness to that.
t.mubarakpuri: He said: "Nay, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them and to that I am one of the witnesses."
t.qarib: He said, "[No], rather, your Lord, who created them, is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, and I am a witness to that
t.wahiduddin: Abraham replied, "Your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, who created them, and I bear witness to that
t.shakir: He said: Nay! your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who brought them into existence, and I am of those who bear witness to this
t.haleem: He said, ‘Listen! Your true Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, He who created them, and I am a witness to this
t.maariful: He said, “No, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, who has created them, and I am one of those who bear witness to it
t.khattab: He replied, “In fact, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Who created them ˹both˺. And to that I bear witness.”
t.ghali: He said, "No indeed, (but) your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth Who originated them, (2) and I am of the ones who testify to this
t.irving: He said: "Rather your Lord is the Lord of Heaven and Earth, and the One Who originated them, while I am another witness for that
t.iwq: He said, "No, rather, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth who created them, and I, to that, am of those who testify

t_id.kemenag: Ibrahim berkata: "Sebenarnya Tuhan kamu ialah Tuhan langit dan bumi yang telah menciptakannya: dan aku termasuk orang-orang yang dapat memberikan bukti atas yang demikian itu".
t_id.sabiq: Ibrâhîm menjawab, "Aku tidak main-main dalam ucapanku tadi. Tuhan yang pantas kalian sembah dengan penuh pengagungan dan kekhusyukan adalah Allah, Tuhan yang menciptakan langit dan bumi tanpa ada contoh sebelumnya. Hanya Dialah yang pantas disembah. Dan aku termasuk orang-orang yang yakin, yang hanya berkata tentang apa yang dilihat dan diketahui."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim berkata, "Sebenarnya Rabb kalian) yang berhak untuk disembah (ialah Rabb) yang memiliki (langit dan bumi yang telah menciptakannya) tanpa contoh sebelumnya (dan aku atas yang demikian itu) yang telah aku katakan sekarang ini (termasuk orang-orang yang menyaksikan") nya.
t_id.iwq: Ibrahim berkata: "Sebenarnya Tuhan kamu ialah Tuhan langit dan bumi yang telah menciptakannya: dan aku termasuk orang-orang yang dapat memberikan bukti atas yang demikian itu".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال بل ربكم» المستحق للعبادة «رب» مالك «السماوات والأرض الذي فطرهن» خلقهن على غير مثال سبق «وأنا على ذلكم» الذي قلته «من الشاهدين» به
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ بَل رَّبُّكُمۡ رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّذِي فَطَرَهُنَّ وَأَنَا۠ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكُم مِّنَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِينَ (فَطَرَهُنَّ: خَلَقَهُنَّ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim berkata, "Sebenarnya Rabb kalian) yang berhak untuk disembah (ialah Rabb) yang memiliki (langit dan bumi yang telah menciptakannya) tanpa contoh sebelumnya (dan aku atas yang demikian itu) yang telah aku katakan sekarang ini (termasuk orang-orang yang menyaksikan") nya

ar: وَتَٱللَّهِ لَأَكِیدَنَّ أَصۡنَـٰمَكُم بَعۡدَ أَن تُوَلُّوا۟ مُدۡبِرِینَ
ar_simple: وَتَاللَّهِ لَأَكِيدَنَّ أَصْنَامَكُمْ بَعْدَ أَنْ تُوَلُّوا مُدْبِرِينَ

t.sahih-int: And [I swear] by Allah, I will surely plan against your idols after you have turned and gone away
t.asad: And [he added to himself.] “By God, I shall most certainly bring about the downfall of your idols as soon as you have turned your backs and gone away!”
t.yusuf-ali: And by Allah, I have a plan for your idols - after ye go away and turn your backs
t.pickthall: And, by Allah, I shall circumvent your idols after ye have gone away and turned your backs
t.arberry: And, by God, I shall assuredly outwit your idols, after you have gone away turning your backs
t.hilali-khan: And by Allah, I shall plot a plan (to destroy) your idols after you have gone away and turned your backs
t.itani: “By God, I will have a plan for your statues after you have gone away.”
t.mubarakpuri: "And by Allah, I shall plot a plan for your idols after you have gone away and turned your backs."
t.qarib: And, by God, I will surely plot against your idols after You have turned your backs and gone away
t.wahiduddin: By the Lord, I will devise a plan against your deities after you have gone away and turned your backs
t.shakir: And, by Allah! I will certainly do something against your idols after you go away, turning back
t.haleem: By God I shall certainly plot against your idols as soon as you have turned your backs!’
t.maariful: And I swear by Allah that I will devise something against your idols after you have gone away turning your backs.”
t.khattab: ˹Then he said to himself,˺ “By Allah! I will surely plot against your idols after you have turned your backs and gone away.”
t.ghali: And by Allah, indeed I will definitely plot against your idols after you have withdrawn, turning away
t.irving: By God, I am planning to confound your idols once you have turned your backs
t.iwq: And I swear by Allah, I will surely plan against your idols after you have turned and gone away

t_id.kemenag: Demi Allah, sesungguhnya aku akan melakukan tipu daya terhadap berhala-berhalamu sesudah kamu pergi meninggalkannya.
t_id.sabiq: Ibrâhîm berkata kepada dirinya sendiri, "Aku bersumpah demi Allah, akan mengatur siasat untuk menghancurkan patung-patung kalian setelah kalian pergi jauh meninggalkannya agar tampak sesatnya apa yang kalian lakukan."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Demi Allah, sesungguhnya aku akan melakukan tipu daya terhadap berhala-berhala kalian sesudah kalian pergi meninggalkannya).
t_id.iwq: Demi Allah, sesungguhnya aku akan melakukan tipu daya terhadap berhala-berhalamu sesudah kamu pergi meninggalkannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وتالله لأكيدن أصنامهم بعد أن تولوا مدبرين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَتَٱللَّهِ لَأَكِيدَنَّ أَصۡنَٰمَكُم بَعۡدَ أَن تُوَلُّواْ مُدۡبِرِينَ (لَأَكِيدَنَّ: لَأَمْكُرَنَّ، وَأُكَسِّرَنَّ, مُدْبِرِينَ: ذَاهِبِينَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Demi Allah, sesungguhnya aku akan melakukan tipu daya terhadap berhala-berhala kalian sesudah kalian pergi meninggalkannya

ar: فَجَعَلَهُمۡ جُذَ ٰذًا إِلَّا كَبِیرࣰا لَّهُمۡ لَعَلَّهُمۡ إِلَیۡهِ یَرۡجِعُونَ
ar_simple: فَجَعَلَهُمْ جُذَاذًا إِلَّا كَبِيرًا لَهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِ يَرْجِعُونَ

t.sahih-int: So he made them into fragments, except a large one among them, that they might return to it [and question]
t.asad: And then he broke those [idols] to pieces, [all] save the biggest of them, so that they might [be able to] turn to it
t.yusuf-ali: So he broke them to pieces, (all) but the biggest of them, that they might turn (and address themselves) to it
t.pickthall: Then he reduced them to fragments, all save the chief of them, that haply they might have recourse to it
t.arberry: So he broke them into fragments, all but a great one they had, for haply they would return to it
t.hilali-khan: So he broke them to pieces, (all) except the biggest of them, that they might turn to it
t.itani: So he reduced them into pieces, except for their biggest, that they may return to it.
t.mubarakpuri: So he broke them to pieces, except the biggest of them, that they might turn to it.
t.qarib: So he broke them into pieces, except for the largest one among them, so that they might return to it [and question]
t.wahiduddin: He broke them all into pieces, except for the biggest one of them, so that they might return to it [for enquiry]
t.shakir: So he broke them into pieces, except the chief of them, that haply they may return to it
t.haleem: He broke them all into pieces, but left the biggest one for them to return to
t.maariful: Then, he turned all of them into pieces, except the big one of them, so that they may come back to it
t.khattab: So he smashed them into pieces, except the biggest of them, so they might turn to it ˹for answers˺
t.ghali: So he made them into scraps, except a great one they had, that possibly they would return to it
t.irving: So he broke them into fragments except for the biggest one of them, so that they might return to [question] it
t.iwq: So he made them into fragments, except a large one among them, that they might return to it and question

t_id.kemenag: Maka Ibrahim membuat berhala-berhala itu hancur berpotong-potong, kecuali yang terbesar (induk) dari patung-patung yang lain; agar mereka kembali (untuk bertanya) kepadanya.
t_id.sabiq: Setelah mereka pergi meninggalkan patung-patung itu, Ibrâhîm mendatanginya lalu menghancurkannya berkeping-keping kecuali patung yang besar agar nantinya menjadi tempat bertanya bagi mereka tentang apa yang telah terjadi pada tuhan-tuhan mereka itu. Tentu, patung besar itu pun tidak akan dapat menjawab apa-apa. Dengan demikian kebatilan akan tampak jelas pada mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Ibrahim membuat berhala-berhala itu) sesudah mereka pergi meninggalkannya menuju ke tempat pertemuan di hari raya mereka (menjadi puing-puing) dapat dibaca Judzaadzan dan Jidzaadzan, artinya hancur terpotong-potong di kapak oleh Nabi Ibrahim (kecuali yang terbesar dari mereka) lalu Nabi Ibrahim menggantungkan kapaknya ke pundak berhala yang terbesar itu (agar mereka kepadanya) yakni kepada berhala yang terbesar itu (menanyakannya) maka mereka akan melihat apa yang ia perbuat terhadap berhala-berhala yang lain.
t_id.iwq: Maka Ibrahim membuat berhala-berhala itu hancur berpotong-potong, kecuali yang terbesar (induk) dari patung-patung yang lain; agar mereka kembali (untuk bertanya) kepadanya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فجعلهم» بعد ذهابهم إلى مجتمعهم في يوم عيد لهم «جُذاذاً» بضم الجيم وكسرها: فتاتاً بفأس «إلا كبيراً لهم» علق الفأس في عنقه «لعلهم إليه» أي إلى الكبير «يرجعون» فيرون ما فعل بغيره
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَجَعَلَهُمۡ جُذَٰذًا إِلَّا كَبِيرٗا لَّهُمۡ لَعَلَّهُمۡ إِلَيۡهِ يَرۡجِعُونَ (جُذَاذًا: قِطَعًا صَغِيرَةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Ibrahim membuat berhala-berhala itu) sesudah mereka pergi meninggalkannya menuju ke tempat pertemuan di hari raya mereka (menjadi puing-puing) dapat dibaca Judzaadzan dan Jidzaadzan, artinya hancur terpotong-potong di kapak oleh Nabi Ibrahim (kecuali yang terbesar dari mereka) lalu Nabi Ibrahim menggantungkan kapaknya ke pundak berhala yang terbesar itu (agar mereka kepadanya) yakni kepada berhala yang terbesar itu (menanyakannya) maka mereka akan melihat apa yang ia perbuat terhadap berhala-berhala yang lain

ar: قَالُوا۟ مَن فَعَلَ هَـٰذَا بِءَالِهَتِنَاۤ إِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِینَ
ar_simple: قَالُوا مَنْ فَعَلَ هَٰذَا بِآلِهَتِنَا إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ

t.sahih-int: They said, "Who has done this to our gods? Indeed, he is of the wrongdoers
t.asad: [When they saw what had happened,] they said: “Who has done this to our gods? Verily, one of the worst wrongdoers is he!”
t.yusuf-ali: They said, "Who has done this to our gods? He must indeed be some man of impiety
t.pickthall: They said: Who hath done this to our gods? Surely it must be some evil-doer
t.arberry: They said, 'Who has. done this with our gods? Surely he is one of the evildoers
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Who has done this to our aliha (gods)? He must indeed be one of the wrong-doers
t.itani: They said, “Who did this to our gods? He is certainly one of the wrongdoers.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Who has done this to our gods He must indeed be one of the wrongdoers."
t.qarib: They said, "Who has done this to our gods? He is unjust
t.wahiduddin: Who has done this to our deities? He must be a wrongdoer
t.shakir: They said: Who has done this to our gods? Most surely he is one of the unjust
t.haleem: They said, ‘Who has done this to our gods? How wicked he must be!’
t.maariful: They said, “Who has done this to our gods? He is one of the wrongdoers indeed.”
t.khattab: They protested, “Who dared do this to our gods? It must be an evildoer!”
t.ghali: They said, "Who has perfomed this with our gods? Surely he is indeed of the unjust
t.irving: They said: "Who did this to our gods? He must be some wrongdoer
t.iwq: And they said, "Who has done this to our gods? Indeed, he is of the wrongdoers

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berkata: "Siapakah yang melakukan perbuatan ini terhadap tuhan-tuhan kami, sesungguhnya dia termasuk orang-orang yang zalim".
t_id.sabiq: Setelah mereka melihat apa yang terjadi pada tuhan-tuhan mereka itu, mereka pun berkata bahwa siapa yang melakukan hal ini terhadap tuhan-tuhan mereka sungguh termasuk orang yang menzalimi diri sendiri dengan menjerumuskannya untuk disiksa.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata) setelah kembali dan melihat apa yang telah diperbuat terhadap berhala-berhala mereka, ("Siapakah yang melakukan perbuatan ini terhadap tuhan-tuhan kami, sesungguhnya dia termasuk orang-orang yang zalim") di dalam perbuatannya ini.
t_id.iwq: Mereka berkata: "Siapakah yang melakukan perbuatan ini terhadap tuhan-tuhan kami, sesungguhnya dia termasuk orang-orang yang zalim".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا» بعد رجوعهم ورؤيتهم ما فعل «من فعل هذا بآلهتنا إنه لمن الظالمين» فيه
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ مَن فَعَلَ هَٰذَا بِـَٔالِهَتِنَآ إِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata) setelah kembali dan melihat apa yang telah diperbuat terhadap berhala-berhala mereka, ("Siapakah yang melakukan perbuatan ini terhadap tuhan-tuhan kami, sesungguhnya dia termasuk orang-orang yang zalim") di dalam perbuatannya ini

ar: قَالُوا۟ سَمِعۡنَا فَتࣰى یَذۡكُرُهُمۡ یُقَالُ لَهُۥۤ إِبۡرَ ٰهِیمُ
ar_simple: قَالُوا سَمِعْنَا فَتًى يَذْكُرُهُمْ يُقَالُ لَهُ إِبْرَاهِيمُ

t.sahih-int: They said, "We heard a young man mention them who is called Abraham
t.asad: Said some [of them]: “We heard a youth speak of these [gods with scorn]: he is called Abraham.”
t.yusuf-ali: They said, "We heard a youth talk of them: He is called Abraham
t.pickthall: They said: We heard a youth make mention of them, who is called Abraham
t.arberry: They said, 'We heard a young man making mention of them, and he was called Abraham
t.hilali-khan: They said: "We heard a young man talking (against) them who is called Ibrahim (Abraham)
t.itani: They said, “We heard a youth mentioning them. He is called Abraham.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "We heard a young man talking against them, who is called Ibrahim."
t.qarib: Some said, "We heard a young man mention them; they call him Abraham
t.wahiduddin: Some said, "We heard a young man, called Abraham, talking about them
t.shakir: They said: We heard a youth called Ibrahim speak of them
t.haleem: Some said, ‘We heard a youth called Abraham talking about them.’
t.maariful: Some of them said, “We have heard a youth talking about them. He is called Ibrāhīm.”
t.khattab: Some said, “We heard a young man, called Abraham, speaking ˹ill˺ of them.”
t.ghali: They said, "We heard a young man making mention of them; he is called Ibrahim." (Abraham)
t.irving: They said: "We heard a young man mentioning them; he is called Abraham
t.iwq: And they said, "We heard a young man mention them who is called Abraham

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berkata: "Kami dengar ada seorang pemuda yang mencela berhala-berhala ini yang bernama Ibrahim".
t_id.sabiq: Di antara mereka ada yang berkata, "Kami mendengar ada seorang anak muda bernama Ibrâhîm yang menghina tuhan-tuhan kita."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata) sebagian dari mereka kepada yang lain, ("Kami dengar ada seorang pemuda yang menyebut-nyebut mereka) yakni yang mencaci maki berhala-berhala itu (yang bernama Ibrahim")
t_id.iwq: Mereka berkata: "Kami dengar ada seorang pemuda yang mencela berhala-berhala ini yang bernama Ibrahim".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا» أي بعضهم لبعض «سمعنا فتى يذكرهم» أي يعيبهم «يقال له إبراهيم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ سَمِعۡنَا فَتٗى يَذۡكُرُهُمۡ يُقَالُ لَهُۥٓ إِبۡرَٰهِيمُ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata) sebagian dari mereka kepada yang lain, ("Kami dengar ada seorang pemuda yang menyebut-nyebut mereka) yakni yang mencaci maki berhala-berhala itu (yang bernama Ibrahim)

ar: قَالُوا۟ فَأۡتُوا۟ بِهِۦ عَلَىٰۤ أَعۡیُنِ ٱلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ یَشۡهَدُونَ
ar_simple: قَالُوا فَأْتُوا بِهِ عَلَىٰ أَعْيُنِ النَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَشْهَدُونَ

t.sahih-int: They said, "Then bring him before the eyes of the people that they may testify
t.asad: [The others] said: “Then bring him before the peoples eyes, so that they might bear witness [against him]!”
t.yusuf-ali: They said, "Then bring him before the eyes of the people, that they may bear witness
t.pickthall: They said: Then bring him (hither) before the people's eyes that they may testify
t.arberry: They said, 'Bring him before the people's eyes; haply they shall bear witness
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Then bring him before the eyes of the people, that they may testify
t.itani: They said, “Bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may witness.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Then bring him before the eyes of the people, that they may testify."
t.qarib: They said, "Then bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may be witnesses
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Then bring him here in the sight of all the people, so that they may act as witnesses
t.shakir: Said they: Then bring him before the eyes of the people, perhaps they may bear witness
t.haleem: They said, ‘Bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may witness [his trial].’
t.maariful: They said, “Then, bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may see”
t.khattab: They demanded, “Bring him before the eyes of the people, so that they may witness ˹his trial˺.”
t.ghali: They said, "Then come up with him (i.e., bring him) before the eyes of the multitude, that possibly they would testify
t.irving: They said: "Bring him before the people´s eyes so they may witness it
t.iwq: And they said, "Then bring him before the eyes of the people that they may testify

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berkata: "(Kalau demikian) bawalah dia dengan cara yang dapat dilihat orang banyak, agar mereka menyaksikan".
t_id.sabiq: Pembesar-pembesar mereka berkata, "Carilah ia dan datangkan ke sini untuk diperhitungkan di hadapan orang banyak, agar mereka menyaksikan apa yang telah ia lakukan!"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Kalau demikian, bawalah dia ke hadapan orang banyak) perlihatkanlah dia kepada mereka (agar mereka menyaksikan") bahwasanya dialah yang telah melakukan semuanya ini.
t_id.iwq: Mereka berkata: "(Kalau demikian) bawalah dia dengan cara yang dapat dilihat orang banyak, agar mereka menyaksikan".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا فأتوا به على أعين الناس» أي ظاهراً «لعلهم يشهدون» عليه أنه الفاعل
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ فَأۡتُواْ بِهِۦ عَلَىٰٓ أَعۡيُنِ ٱلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَشۡهَدُونَ (عَلَى أَعْيُنِ النَّاسِ: بِمَرْأًى مِنَ النَّاسِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Kalau demikian, bawalah dia ke hadapan orang banyak) perlihatkanlah dia kepada mereka (agar mereka menyaksikan") bahwasanya dialah yang telah melakukan semuanya ini

ar: قَالُوۤا۟ ءَأَنتَ فَعَلۡتَ هَـٰذَا بِءَالِهَتِنَا یَـٰۤإِبۡرَ ٰهِیمُ
ar_simple: قَالُوا أَأَنْتَ فَعَلْتَ هَٰذَا بِآلِهَتِنَا يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ

t.sahih-int: They said, "Have you done this to our gods, O Abraham
t.asad: [And when he came.] they asked: “Hast thou done this to our gods, O Abraham?”
t.yusuf-ali: They said, "Art thou the one that did this with our gods, O Abraham
t.pickthall: They said: Is it thou who hast done this to our gods, O Abraham
t.arberry: They said, 'So, art thou the man who did this unto our gods, Abraham
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Are you the one who has done this to our gods, O Ibrahim (Abraham)
t.itani: They said, “Are you the one who did this to our gods, O Abraham?”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Are you the one who has done this to our gods, O Ibrahim"
t.qarib: They said, "Have you done this to our gods, Abraham
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Abraham, was it you who did this to our deities
t.shakir: They said: Have you done this to our gods, O Ibrahim
t.haleem: They asked, ‘Was it you, Abraham, who did this to our gods?’
t.maariful: They said, “Is it you O Ibrāhīm who has done this to our gods?”
t.khattab: They asked, “Was it you who did this to our gods, O Abraham?”
t.ghali: They said, "Is it you who performed this with our gods, O Ibrahim
t.irving: They said: "Is it you who has done this to our gods, Abraham
t.iwq: And they said, "Have you done this to our gods, O Abraham

t_id.kemenag: Mereka bertanya: "Apakah kamu, yang melakukan perbuatan ini terhadap tuhan-tuhan kami, hai Ibrahim?"
t_id.sabiq: Setelah Ibrâhîm berhasil didatangkan, mereka berkata, "Kamukah yang melakukan ini semua terhadap tuhan-tuhan kami, Ibrâhîm?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka bertanya) setelah menghadirkan Ibrahim, ("Apakah kamu) dapat dibaca A'anta dan A-anta (yang melakukan perbuatan ini terhadap tuhan-tuhan kami, hai Ibrahim?")
t_id.iwq: Mereka bertanya: "Apakah kamu, yang melakukan perbuatan ini terhadap tuhan-tuhan kami, hai Ibrahim?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا» له بعد إتيانه «أأنت» بتحقيق الهمزتين وإبدال الثانية ألفاً وتسهيلها وإدخال ألف بين المسهلة والأخرى وتركه «فعلت هذا بآلهتنا يا إبراهيم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُوٓاْ ءَأَنتَ فَعَلۡتَ هَٰذَا بِـَٔالِهَتِنَا يَـٰٓإِبۡرَٰهِيمُ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka bertanya) setelah menghadirkan Ibrahim, ("Apakah kamu) dapat dibaca A'anta dan A-anta (yang melakukan perbuatan ini terhadap tuhan-tuhan kami, hai Ibrahim)

ar: قَالَ بَلۡ فَعَلَهُۥ كَبِیرُهُمۡ هَـٰذَا فَسۡءَلُوهُمۡ إِن كَانُوا۟ یَنطِقُونَ
ar_simple: قَالَ بَلْ فَعَلَهُ كَبِيرُهُمْ هَٰذَا فَاسْأَلُوهُمْ إِنْ كَانُوا يَنْطِقُونَ

t.sahih-int: He said, "Rather, this - the largest of them - did it, so ask them, if they should [be able to] speak
t.asad: He answered: “Nay, it was this one, the biggest of them, that did it: but ask them [yourselves] - provided they can speak!”
t.yusuf-ali: He said: "Nay, this was done by - this is their biggest one! ask them, if they can speak intelligently
t.pickthall: He said: But this, their chief hath done it. So question them, if they can speak
t.arberry: He said, 'No; it was this great one of them that did it. Question them; if they are able to speak
t.hilali-khan: Ibrahim (Abraham)] said: "Nay, this one, the biggest of them (idols) did it. Ask them, if they can speak
t.itani: He said, “But it was this biggest of them that did it. Ask them, if they can speak.”
t.mubarakpuri: He said: "Nay, this one, the biggest of them did it. Ask them, if they can speak!"
t.qarib: He said, "Rather, this one, the largest of them, did it, so ask them, if they are able to speak
t.wahiduddin: He answered, "Rather this biggest one of them did it. Ask them, if they can speak
t.shakir: He said: Surely (some doer) has done it; the chief of them is this, therefore ask them, if they can speak
t.haleem: He said, ‘No, it was done by the biggest of them- this one. Ask them, if they can talk.’
t.maariful: He said, “Rather, this is done by this chief of theirs. So, ask them if they can speak.”
t.khattab: He replied ˹sarcastically˺, “No, this one—the biggest of them—did it! So ask them, if they can talk!”
t.ghali: He said, "No indeed, it was this great one of them that performed it; so, ask them in case they (can) pronounce (a word)
t.irving: He said: "Rather this biggest one of them did it. Ask them, if they are able to speak up
t.iwq: He said, "Rather, this - the largest of them - did it, so ask them, if they should be able to speak

t_id.kemenag: Ibrahim menjawab: "Sebenarnya patung yang besar itulah yang melakukannya, maka tanyakanlah kepada berhala itu, jika mereka dapat berbicara".
t_id.sabiq: Dengan maksud mengingatkan kesesatan mereka, Ibrâhîm menjawab, "Tuhan terbesar itulah yang melakukannya. Tanyakan saja kepada tuhan-tuhan yang lain tentang siapa yang telah melakukan hal itu kalau mereka bisa menjawab pertanyaan kalian!"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim menjawab) seraya menyembunyikan apa yang sebenarnya telah ia lakukan, ("Sebenarnya patung yang besar itulah yang melakukannya, maka tanyakanlah kepada berhala-berhala itu) siapakah pelakunya (jika mereka dapat berbicara") Jawab syarat dari kalimat ayat ini telah didahulukan dan kalimat yang sebelumnya merupakan sindiran bagi para penyembah berhala, bahwa berhala-berhala yang telah dimaklumi ketidakmampuannya untuk berbuat itu bukan tuhan.
t_id.iwq: Ibrahim menjawab: "Sebenarnya patung yang besar itulah yang melakukannya, maka tanyakanlah kepada berhala itu, jika mereka dapat berbicara".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» ساكتاً عن فعله «بل فعله كبيرهم هذا فاسألوهم» عن فاعله «إن كانوا ينطقون» فيه تقديم جواب الشرط وفيها قبله تعرض لهم بأن الصنم المعلوم عجزه عن الفعل لا يكون إلهاً
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ بَلۡ فَعَلَهُۥ كَبِيرُهُمۡ هَٰذَا فَسۡـَٔلُوهُمۡ إِن كَانُواْ يَنطِقُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim menjawab) seraya menyembunyikan apa yang sebenarnya telah ia lakukan, ("Sebenarnya patung yang besar itulah yang melakukannya, maka tanyakanlah kepada berhala-berhala itu) siapakah pelakunya (jika mereka dapat berbicara") Jawab syarat dari kalimat ayat ini telah didahulukan dan kalimat yang sebelumnya merupakan sindiran bagi para penyembah berhala, bahwa berhala-berhala yang telah dimaklumi ketidakmampuannya untuk berbuat itu bukan tuhan

ar: فَرَجَعُوۤا۟ إِلَىٰۤ أَنفُسِهِمۡ فَقَالُوۤا۟ إِنَّكُمۡ أَنتُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ
ar_simple: فَرَجَعُوا إِلَىٰ أَنْفُسِهِمْ فَقَالُوا إِنَّكُمْ أَنْتُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ

t.sahih-int: So they returned to [blaming] themselves and said [to each other], "Indeed, you are the wrongdoers
t.asad: And so they turned upon one another, saying, “Behold, it is you who are doing wrong.”
t.yusuf-ali: So they turned to themselves and said, "Surely ye are the ones in the wrong
t.pickthall: Then gathered they apart and said: Lo! ye yourselves are the wrong-doers
t.arberry: So they returned one to another, and they said, 'Surely it is you who are the evildoers
t.hilali-khan: So they turned to themselves and said: "Verily, you are the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers)
t.itani: Then they turned to one another, and said, “You yourselves are the wrongdoers.”
t.mubarakpuri: So they turned to themselves and said: "Verily, you are the wrongdoers."
t.qarib: So they turned on one another, saying, "It is you who are the unjust
t.wahiduddin: Then they turned to one another and said, "It is you yourselves who are in the wrong
t.shakir: Then they turned to themselves and said: Surely you yourselves are the unjust
t.haleem: They turned to one another, saying, ‘It is you who are in the wrong,’
t.maariful: So they turned to one another and said, “In fact, you yourselves are the wrongdoers.”
t.khattab: So they came back to their senses, saying ˹to one another˺, “You yourselves are truly the wrongdoers!”
t.ghali: So they returned one to another (Literally: returned to themselves) (and) then said, "Surely you yourselves are the unjust
t.irving: They turned to one another and said: "You yourselves are the wrongdoers
t.iwq: So they returned to blaming themselves and said to each other, "Indeed, you are the wrongdoers

t_id.kemenag: Maka mereka telah kembali kepada kesadaran dan lalu berkata: "Sesungguhnya kamu sekalian adalah orang-orang yang menganiaya (diri sendiri)",
t_id.sabiq: Mereka pun menengok diri sendiri yang menyembah sesuatu yang tidak dapat mendatangkan keuntungan dan mencegah kejahatan. Mereka menyadari kesalahannya. Sebagian mereka ada yang berkata, "Ibrâhîm tidaklah zalim. Kalianlah yang zalim dengan menyembah sesuatu yang tidak pantas disembah."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka telah kembali kepada kesadaran mereka) setelah berpikir (lalu berkata) kepada diri mereka sendiri, ("Sesungguhnya kalian adalah orang-orang yang berbuat aniaya") disebabkan kalian menyembah berhala yang tidak dapat berbicara.
t_id.iwq: Maka mereka telah kembali kepada kesadaran dan lalu berkata: "Sesungguhnya kamu sekalian adalah orang-orang yang menganiaya (diri sendiri)",

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فرجعوا إلى أنفسهم» بالتفكر «فقالوا» لأنفسهم «إنكم أنتم الظالمون» بعبادتكم من لا ينطق
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَرَجَعُوٓاْ إِلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمۡ فَقَالُوٓاْ إِنَّكُمۡ أَنتُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka mereka telah kembali kepada kesadaran mereka) setelah berpikir (lalu berkata) kepada diri mereka sendiri, ("Sesungguhnya kalian adalah orang-orang yang berbuat aniaya") disebabkan kalian menyembah berhala yang tidak dapat berbicara

ar: ثُمَّ نُكِسُوا۟ عَلَىٰ رُءُوسِهِمۡ لَقَدۡ عَلِمۡتَ مَا هَـٰۤؤُلَاۤءِ یَنطِقُونَ
ar_simple: ثُمَّ نُكِسُوا عَلَىٰ رُءُوسِهِمْ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ مَا هَٰؤُلَاءِ يَنْطِقُونَ

t.sahih-int: Then they reversed themselves, [saying], "You have already known that these do not speak
t.asad: But then they relapsed into their former way of thinking and said: “Thou knowest very well that these [idols] cannot speak!”
t.yusuf-ali: Then were they confounded with shame: (they said), "Thou knowest full well that these (idols) do not speak
t.pickthall: And they were utterly confounded, and they said: Well thou knowest that these speak not
t.arberry: Then they were utterly put to confusion saying, 'Very well indeed thou knowest these do not speak
t.hilali-khan: Then they turned to themselves (their first thought and said): "Indeed you [Ibrahim (Abraham)] know well that these (idols) speak not
t.itani: But they reverted to their old ideas: “You certainly know that these do not speak.”
t.mubarakpuri: Then they turned to themselves: "Indeed you know well that these speak not!"
t.qarib: Then, they reversed themselves, [saying], "You [Abraham] knew that these [idols] do not speak
t.wahiduddin: then they hung their heads, and said, "O Abraham! You know they cannot speak
t.shakir: Then they were made to hang down their heads: Certainly you know that they do not speak
t.haleem: but then they lapsed again and said, ‘You know very well these gods cannot speak.’
t.maariful: Then, hanging their heads they reversed their position (and replied to Ibrāhīm,) “You already knew that they do not speak.”
t.khattab: Then they ˹quickly˺ regressed to their ˹original˺ mind-set, ˹arguing,˺ “You already know that those ˹idols˺ cannot talk.”
t.ghali: Thereafter they were made to relapse (Literally: they were turned upon their heads) (into idolatry), (saying), "Indeed you already know that these (can) in no way pronounce (a word)
t.irving: Then they hung their heads: "You knew those things do not utter [a word]
t.iwq: Then they reversed themselves, saying, "You have already known that these do not speak

t_id.kemenag: kemudian kepala mereka jadi tertunduk (lalu berkata): "Sesungguhnya kamu (hai Ibrahim) telah mengetahui bahwa berhala-berhala itu tidak dapat berbicara".
t_id.sabiq: Tetapi mereka kemudian kembali terbawa oleh kesesatannya setelah sebelumnya sadar. Mereka bekata kepada Ibrâhîm, "Kamu tahu bahwa patung-patung yang kami sembah itu tidak dapat berbicara, tapi mengapa kamu menyuruh kami bertanya kepada mereka?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian mereka menundukkan) karena malu kepada Allah (kepala mereka) karena kekafirannya telah dinyatakan, maka mereka berkata, "Demi Allah! (Sesungguhnya kamu telah mengetahui, bahwa berhala-berhala itu tidak dapat berbicara") mengapa kamu menyuruh kami bertanya kepada mereka.
t_id.iwq: kemudian kepala mereka jadi tertunduk (lalu berkata): "Sesungguhnya kamu (hai Ibrahim) telah mengetahui bahwa berhala-berhala itu tidak dapat berbicara".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ثم نكسوا» من الله «على رءُوسهم» أي ردوا إلى كفرهم وقالوا والله «لقد علمتَ ما هؤلاء ينطقون» أي فكيف تأمرنا بسؤالهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: ثُمَّ نُكِسُواْ عَلَىٰ رُءُوسِهِمۡ لَقَدۡ عَلِمۡتَ مَا هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ يَنطِقُونَ (نُكِسُوا عَلَى رُؤُوسِهِمْ: رَجَعُوا إِلَى عِنَادِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kemudian mereka menundukkan) karena malu kepada Allah (kepala mereka) karena kekafirannya telah dinyatakan, maka mereka berkata, "Demi Allah! (Sesungguhnya kamu telah mengetahui, bahwa berhala-berhala itu tidak dapat berbicara") mengapa kamu menyuruh kami bertanya kepada mereka

ar: قَالَ أَفَتَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا یَنفَعُكُمۡ شَیۡءࣰا وَلَا یَضُرُّكُمۡ
ar_simple: قَالَ أَفَتَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنْفَعُكُمْ شَيْئًا وَلَا يَضُرُّكُمْ

t.sahih-int: He said, "Then do you worship instead of Allah that which does not benefit you at all or harm you
t.asad: Said [Abraham]: “Do you then worship, instead of God, something that cannot benefit you in any way, nor harm you
t.yusuf-ali: (Abraham) said, "Do ye then worship, besides Allah, things that can neither be of any good to you nor do you harm
t.pickthall: He said: Worship ye then instead of Allah that which cannot profit you at all, nor harm you
t.arberry: He said, 'What, and do you serve, apart from God, that which profits you nothing; neither hurts you
t.hilali-khan: Ibrahim (Abraham)] said: "Do you then worship besides Allah, things that can neither profit you, nor harm you
t.itani: He said, “Do you worship, instead of God, what can neither benefit you in anything, nor harm you?
t.mubarakpuri: He said: "Do you then worship besides Allah, things that can neither profit you nor harm you"
t.qarib: He said, "Then do you worship what does not help or harm you at all instead of God
t.wahiduddin: Abraham said, "So, do you worship something instead of God that can neither benefit you nor harm you
t.shakir: He said: What! do you then serve besides Allah what brings you not any benefit at all, nor does it harm you
t.haleem: Abraham said, ‘How can you worship what can neither benefit nor harm you, instead of God
t.maariful: He said, “Do you then worship, beside Allah, what does neither benefit you in the least nor harm you
t.khattab: He rebuked ˹them˺, “Do you then worship—instead of Allah—what can neither benefit nor harm you in any way
t.ghali: He said, "Do you then worship, apart from Allah, that which does not profit you anything nor harms you
t.irving: He said: "So do you worship something instead of God that neither benefits you in any way nor does it harm you
t.iwq: And he said, "Then do you worship instead of Allah that which does not benefit you at all or harm you

t_id.kemenag: Ibrahim berkata: Maka mengapakah kamu menyembah selain Allah sesuatu yang tidak dapat memberi manfaat sedikitpun dan tidak (pula) memberi mudharat kepada kamu?"
t_id.sabiq: Ibrâhîm berkata, "Selemah itukah keadaan patung-patung itu? Dan seperti itu pulakah sikap kalian terhadapnya, hingga kalian menyembah sesuatu selain Allah yang tidak akan menguntungkan bila kalian sembah dan tidak merugikan bila kalian abaikan?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim berkata, "Maka mengapakah kalian menyembah selain Allah) sebagai ganti dari-Nya untuk kalian sembah (sesuatu yang tidak dapat memberi manfaat sedikit pun kepada kalian) berupa rezeki dan lain-lainnya (dan tidak pula memberi mudarat kepada kalian?") barang sedikit pun, jika kalian tidak menyembahnya.
t_id.iwq: Ibrahim berkata: Maka mengapakah kamu menyembah selain Allah sesuatu yang tidak dapat memberi manfaat sedikitpun dan tidak (pula) memberi mudharat kepada kamu?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال أفتعبدون من دون الله» أي بدله «ما لا ينفعكم شيئاً» من رزق وغيره «ولا يضركم» شيئاً إذا لم تعبدوه
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ أَفَتَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُكُمۡ شَيۡـٔٗا وَلَا يَضُرُّكُمۡ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim berkata, "Maka mengapakah kalian menyembah selain Allah) sebagai ganti dari-Nya untuk kalian sembah (sesuatu yang tidak dapat memberi manfaat sedikit pun kepada kalian) berupa rezeki dan lain-lainnya (dan tidak pula memberi mudarat kepada kalian?") barang sedikit pun, jika kalian tidak menyembahnya

ar: أُفࣲّ لَّكُمۡ وَلِمَا تَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِۚ أَفَلَا تَعۡقِلُونَ
ar_simple: أُفٍّ لَكُمْ وَلِمَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ ۖ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ

t.sahih-int: Uff to you and to what you worship instead of Allah. Then will you not use reason
t.asad: Fie upon you and upon all that you worship instead of God! Will you not, then, use your reason?”
t.yusuf-ali: Fie upon you, and upon the things that ye worship besides Allah! Have ye no sense
t.pickthall: Fie on you and all that ye worship instead of Allah! Have ye then no sense
t.arberry: Fie upon you and that you serve apart from God! Do you not understand
t.hilali-khan: Fie upon you, and upon that which you worship besides Allah! Have you then no sense
t.itani: Fie on you, and on what you worship instead of God. Do you not understand?”
t.mubarakpuri: "Fie upon you, and upon that which you worship besides Allah! Have you then no sense"
t.qarib: Shame on you, and shame on what you worship instead of God. Will you not use reason then
t.wahiduddin: Shame on you and on whatever you worship instead of God. Can you not understand
t.shakir: Fie on you and on what you serve besides Allah; what! do you not then understand
t.haleem: Shame on you and on the things you worship instead of God. Have you no sense?’
t.maariful: Fie upon you and upon what you worship other than Allah. Do you then not understand?”
t.khattab: Shame on you and whatever you worship instead of Allah! Do you not have any sense?”
t.ghali: Fie upon you and upon whatever you worship apart from Allah! Do you then not consider
t.irving: Shame on you and on whatever you worship instead of God [Alone]! Don´t you use your reason
t.iwq: Uff to you and to what you worship instead of Allah. Then will you not use reason

t_id.kemenag: Ah (celakalah) kamu dan apa yang kamu sembah selain Allah. Maka apakah kamu tidak memahami?
t_id.sabiq: Alangkah jeleknya kalian dan tuhan-tuhan kalian itu! Apakah kalian tidak mau berpikir dan merenungkan hal-hal yang kalian lihat itu? Patung-patung itu tidak pantas disembah."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ah, alangkah buruknya) lafal Uffin atau Uffan ini bermakna Mashdar, artinya busuklah (kalian beserta apa yang kalian sembah selain Allah. Maka apakah kalian tidak memahami?) bahwa berhala-berhala itu tidak berhak untuk disembah dan tidak layak untuk dijadikan sesembahan, karena sesungguhnya yang berhak disembah itu hanyalah Allah semata.
t_id.iwq: Ah (celakalah) kamu dan apa yang kamu sembah selain Allah. Maka apakah kamu tidak memahami?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أف» بكسر الفاء وفتحها بمعنى مصدر أي نتناً وقبحاً «لكم ولما تعبدون من دون الله» أي غيره «أفلا تعقلون» أن هذه الأصنام لا تستحق العبادة ولا تصلح لها، وإنما يستحقها الله تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: أُفّٖ لَّكُمۡ وَلِمَا تَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِۚ أَفَلَا تَعۡقِلُونَ (أُفٍّ لَّكُمْ: قُبْحًا لَكُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ah, alangkah buruknya) lafal Uffin atau Uffan ini bermakna Mashdar, artinya busuklah (kalian beserta apa yang kalian sembah selain Allah. Maka apakah kalian tidak memahami?) bahwa berhala-berhala itu tidak berhak untuk disembah dan tidak layak untuk dijadikan sesembahan, karena sesungguhnya yang berhak disembah itu hanyalah Allah semata

ar: قَالُوا۟ حَرِّقُوهُ وَٱنصُرُوۤا۟ ءَالِهَتَكُمۡ إِن كُنتُمۡ فَـٰعِلِینَ
ar_simple: قَالُوا حَرِّقُوهُ وَانْصُرُوا آلِهَتَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فَاعِلِينَ

t.sahih-int: They said, "Burn him and support your gods - if you are to act
t.asad: They exclaimed: “Burn him, and [thereby] succour your gods, if you are going to do [anything]!”
t.yusuf-ali: They said, "Burn him and protect your gods, If ye do (anything at all)
t.pickthall: They cried: Burn him and stand by your gods, if ye will be doing
t.arberry: They said, 'Burn him, and help your gods, if you would do aught
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Burn him and help your aliha (gods), if you will be doing
t.itani: They said, “Burn him and support your gods, if you are going to act.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Burn him and help your gods, if you will be doing."
t.qarib: They said, "Burn him and support your gods-if you are to act
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Burn him and help your deities, if you are resolved to do something
t.shakir: They said: Burn him and help your gods, if you are going to do (anything)
t.haleem: They said, ‘Burn him and avenge your gods, if you are going to do the right thing.’
t.maariful: They said, “(O people) burn him and help your gods, if you are to take action.”
t.khattab: They concluded, “Burn him up to avenge your gods, if you must act.”
t.ghali: They said, "Burn him away and vindicate your gods, in case you are performing that
t.irving: They said: "Burn him up and support your gods if you must be doing something
t.iwq: And they said, "Burn him and support your gods - if you are to act

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berkata: "Bakarlah dia dan bantulah tuhan-tuhan kamu, jika kamu benar-benar hendak bertindak".
t_id.sabiq: Terjadilah pembicaraan di antara sesama mereka. Ada yang mengatakan, "Bakar saja dia dengan api. Jadikan hal itu sebagai wujud pembelaan kalian kepada tuhan, bila kalian benar-benar ingin melakukan sesuatu yang bisa menolong tuhan kalian!"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Bakarlah dia) yakni Nabi Ibrahim (dan bantulah tuhan-tuhan kalian) dengan membakar Ibrahim (jika kalian benar-benar hendak bertindak") untuk menolong tuhan-tuhan kalian. Mereka segera mengumpulkan kayu-kayu yang banyak sekali, lalu mereka menyalakannya. Mereka mengikat Nabi Ibrahim, kemudian menaruhnya pada Manjaniq atau alat pelontar yang besar, lalu Nabi Ibrahim dilemparkan ke dalam api yang besar itu. Allah berfirman:
t_id.iwq: Mereka berkata: "Bakarlah dia dan bantulah tuhan-tuhan kamu, jika kamu benar-benar hendak bertindak".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا حرِّقوه» أي إبراهيم «وانصروا آلهتكم» أي بتحريقه «إن كنتم فاعلين» نصرتها فجمعوا له الحطب الكثير وأضرموا النار في جميعه وأوثقوا إبراهيم وجعلوه في منجنيق ورموه في النار قال تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ حَرِّقُوهُ وَٱنصُرُوٓاْ ءَالِهَتَكُمۡ إِن كُنتُمۡ فَٰعِلِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Bakarlah dia) yakni Nabi Ibrahim (dan bantulah tuhan-tuhan kalian) dengan membakar Ibrahim (jika kalian benar-benar hendak bertindak") untuk menolong tuhan-tuhan kalian. Mereka segera mengumpulkan kayu-kayu yang banyak sekali, lalu mereka menyalakannya. Mereka mengikat Nabi Ibrahim, kemudian menaruhnya pada Manjaniq atau alat pelontar yang besar, lalu Nabi Ibrahim dilemparkan ke dalam api yang besar itu. Allah berfirman

ar: قُلۡنَا یَـٰنَارُ كُونِی بَرۡدࣰا وَسَلَـٰمًا عَلَىٰۤ إِبۡرَ ٰهِیمَ
ar_simple: قُلْنَا يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلَامًا عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ

t.sahih-int: Allah said, "O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham
t.asad: [But] We said: O fire! Be thou cool, and [a source of] inner peace for Abraham!”
t.yusuf-ali: We said, "O Fire! be thou cool, and (a means of) safety for Abraham
t.pickthall: We said: O fire, be coolness and peace for Abraham
t.arberry: We said, 'O fire, be coolness and safety for Abraham
t.hilali-khan: We (Allah) said: "O fire! Be you coolness and safety for Ibrahim (Abraham)
t.itani: We said, “O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham.”
t.mubarakpuri: We said: "O fire! Be you cool and safety for Ibrahim!"
t.qarib: But We said, "Fire, be coolness and peace for Abraham
t.wahiduddin: But We said, "Fire! Be cool and a means of safety for Abraham
t.shakir: We said: O fire! be a comfort and peace to Ibrahim
t.haleem: But We said, ‘Fire, be cool and safe for Abraham.’
t.maariful: We said, “O fire, be cold and safe for Ibrāhīm.”
t.khattab: We ordered, “O fire! Be cool and safe for Abraham!”
t.ghali: We said, "O fire, be coolness and peace upon Ibrahim
t.irving: We said: "Fire, be cool, and safe for Abraham
t.iwq: Allah said, "O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham

t_id.kemenag: Kami berfirman: "Hai api menjadi dinginlah, dan menjadi keselamatanlah bagi Ibrahim",
t_id.sabiq: Kemudian Kami membuat api yang membakar Ibrâhîm menjadi dingin, mengandung keselamatan, dan tidak membahayakannya sama sekali.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kami berfirman, "Hai api! Menjadi dinginlah dan menjadi keselamatanlah bagi Ibrahim") maka api itu tidak membakarnya selain pada tali-tali pengikatnya saja dan lenyaplah panas api itu, yang tinggal hanyalah cahayanya saja, hal ini berkat perintah Allah, 'Salaaman' yakni menjadi keselamatan bagi Ibrahim, akhirnya Nabi Ibrahim selamat dari kematian karena api itu dingin.
t_id.iwq: Kami berfirman: "Hai api menjadi dinginlah, dan menjadi keselamatanlah bagi Ibrahim",

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (قلنا يا نار كوني برداً وسلاماً على إبراهيم) فلم تحرق منه غير وثاقه، وذهبت حرارتها وبقيت إضاءتها وبقوله "" وسلاماً "" سلم من الموت ببردها
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُلۡنَا يَٰنَارُ كُونِي بَرۡدٗا وَسَلَٰمًا عَلَىٰٓ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kami berfirman, "Hai api! Menjadi dinginlah dan menjadi keselamatanlah bagi Ibrahim") maka api itu tidak membakarnya selain pada tali-tali pengikatnya saja dan lenyaplah panas api itu, yang tinggal hanyalah cahayanya saja, hal ini berkat perintah Allah, 'Salaaman' yakni menjadi keselamatan bagi Ibrahim, akhirnya Nabi Ibrahim selamat dari kematian karena api itu dingin

ar: وَأَرَادُوا۟ بِهِۦ كَیۡدࣰا فَجَعَلۡنَـٰهُمُ ٱلۡأَخۡسَرِینَ
ar_simple: وَأَرَادُوا بِهِ كَيْدًا فَجَعَلْنَاهُمُ الْأَخْسَرِينَ

t.sahih-int: And they intended for him harm, but We made them the greatest losers
t.asad: and whereas they sought to do evil unto him, We caused them to suffer the greatest loss
t.yusuf-ali: Then they sought a stratagem against him: but We made them the ones that lost most
t.pickthall: And they wished to set a snare for him, but We made them the greater losers
t.arberry: They desired to outwit him; so We made them the worse losers
t.hilali-khan: And they wanted to harm him, but We made them the worst losers
t.itani: They planned to harm him, but We made them the worst losers.
t.mubarakpuri: And they wanted to harm him, but We made them the worst losers.
t.qarib: And they intended to harm him, but We made them the greatest losers
t.wahiduddin: They had sought to do him harm, but We frustrated them
t.shakir: And they desired a war on him, but We made them the greatest losers
t.haleem: They planned to harm him, but We made them suffer the greatest loss
t.maariful: And they intended to harm him, but We made them the worst losers
t.khattab: They had sought to harm him, but We made them the worst losers
t.ghali: And they would (devise) a plot against him; yet We made them the greatest losers
t.irving: They wanted to outwit him, so We made them lose the most
t.iwq: And they intended for him harm, but We made them the greatest losers

t_id.kemenag: mereka hendak berbuat makar terhadap Ibrahim, maka Kami menjadikan mereka itu orang-orang yang paling merugi.
t_id.sabiq: Dengan pembakaran itu, mereka sebetulnya ingin membuat Ibrâhîm mati. Tetapi Kami menyelamatkannya. Dengan demikian, mereka Kami jadikan sebagai sekelompok orang yang paling merugi.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka hendak berbuat makar terhadap Ibrahim) yaitu dengan membakarnya (maka Kami menjadikan mereka itu orang-orang yang paling merugi) di dalam tujuan mereka.
t_id.iwq: mereka hendak berbuat makar terhadap Ibrahim, maka Kami menjadikan mereka itu orang-orang yang paling merugi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وأرادوا به كيداً» وهو التحريق «فجعلناهم الأخسرين» في مرادهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَأَرَادُواْ بِهِۦ كَيۡدٗا فَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمُ ٱلۡأَخۡسَرِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka hendak berbuat makar terhadap Ibrahim) yaitu dengan membakarnya (maka Kami menjadikan mereka itu orang-orang yang paling merugi) di dalam tujuan mereka

ar: وَنَجَّیۡنَـٰهُ وَلُوطًا إِلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّتِی بَـٰرَكۡنَا فِیهَا لِلۡعَـٰلَمِینَ
ar_simple: وَنَجَّيْنَاهُ وَلُوطًا إِلَى الْأَرْضِ الَّتِي بَارَكْنَا فِيهَا لِلْعَالَمِينَ

t.sahih-int: And We delivered him and Lot to the land which We had blessed for the worlds
t.asad: for We saved him and Lot, [his brother’s son, by guiding them] to the land which We have blessed for all times to come
t.yusuf-ali: But We delivered him and (his nephew) Lut (and directed them) to the land which We have blessed for the nations
t.pickthall: And We rescued him and Lot (and brought them) to the land which We have blessed for (all) peoples
t.arberry: and We delivered him, and Lot, unto the land that We had blessed for all beings
t.hilali-khan: And We rescued him and Lout (Lot) to the land which We have blessed for the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns)
t.itani: And We delivered him, and Lot, to the land that We blessed for all people.
t.mubarakpuri: And We rescued him and Lut to the land which We have blessed for the nations.
t.qarib: And We delivered him and Lot to the land which We had blessed for all people
t.wahiduddin: And We saved him and Lot [and brought them] to a land which We had blessed for all people
t.shakir: And We delivered him as well as Lut (removing them) to the land which We had blessed for all people
t.haleem: We saved him and Lot [and sent them] to the land We blessed for all people
t.maariful: And We rescued him and LūT towards the land we blessed for all the worlds
t.khattab: Then We delivered him, along with Lot, to the land We had showered with blessings for all people
t.ghali: And We safely delivered him and Lut (Lot) to the land We have blessed for the worlds
t.irving: We rescued him and Lot, [bringing them] to a land which We had blessed for [everyone in] the Universe
t.iwq: And We delivered him and Lot to the land which We had blessed for the worlds

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami seIamatkan Ibrahim dan Luth ke sebuah negeri yang Kami telah memberkahinya untuk sekalian manusia.
t_id.sabiq: Begitulah, Kami telah menyelamatkan Ibrâhîm dan Lûth dari tipu daya orang-orang yang berbuat makar. Mereka berdua kemudian berpindah ke belahan bumi yang Kami penuhi dengan kebaikan untuk manusia dan tempat Kami mengutus banyak nabi.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami selamatkan Ibrahim dan Luth) anak saudara Nabi Ibrahim yang bernama Haran yang tinggal di negeri Iraq (ke sebuah negeri yang Kami telah memberkahinya untuk sekalian manusia) dengan menjadikan sungai-sungai dan pohon-pohon yang banyak padanya, yaitu negeri Syam. Nabi Ibrahim tinggal di negeri Palestina sedangkan Nabi Luth di Mu'tafikah; jarak antara kedua negeri itu dapat ditempuh dalam sehari.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami seIamatkan Ibrahim dan Luth ke sebuah negeri yang Kami telah memberkahinya untuk sekalian manusia.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ونجيناه ولوطاً» ابن أخيه هاران من العراق «إلى الأرض التي باركنا فيها للعالمين» بكثرة الأنهار والأشجار وهي الشام نزل إبراهيم بفلسطين ولوط بالمؤتفكة وبينهما يوم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَنَجَّيۡنَٰهُ وَلُوطًا إِلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ ٱلَّتِي بَٰرَكۡنَا فِيهَا لِلۡعَٰلَمِينَ (الْأَرْضِ الَّتِي بَارَكْنَا فِيهَا: أَرْضِ الشَّامِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami selamatkan Ibrahim dan Luth) anak saudara Nabi Ibrahim yang bernama Haran yang tinggal di negeri Iraq (ke sebuah negeri yang Kami telah memberkahinya untuk sekalian manusia) dengan menjadikan sungai-sungai dan pohon-pohon yang banyak padanya, yaitu negeri Syam. Nabi Ibrahim tinggal di negeri Palestina sedangkan Nabi Luth di Mu'tafikah; jarak antara kedua negeri itu dapat ditempuh dalam sehari

ar: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥۤ إِسۡحَـٰقَ وَیَعۡقُوبَ نَافِلَةࣰۖ وَكُلࣰّا جَعَلۡنَا صَـٰلِحِینَ
ar_simple: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُ إِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ نَافِلَةً ۖ وَكُلًّا جَعَلْنَا صَالِحِينَ

t.sahih-int: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in addition, and all [of them] We made righteous
t.asad: And We bestowed upon him Isaac and [Isaacs son] Jacob as an additional gift and caused all of them to be righteous men
t.yusuf-ali: And We bestowed on him Isaac and, as an additional gift, (a grandson), Jacob, and We made righteous men of every one (of them)
t.pickthall: And We bestowed upon him Isaac, and Jacob as a grandson. Each of them We made righteous
t.arberry: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in superfluity, and every one made We righteous
t.hilali-khan: And We bestowed upon him Ishaque (Isaac), and (a grandson) Ya'qub (Jacob). Each one We made righteous
t.itani: And We granted him Isaac and Jacob as a gift; and each We made righteous.
t.mubarakpuri: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq, and Ya`qub Nafilatan. Each one We made righteous.
t.qarib: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob as an additional gift, and all [of them] We made righteous
t.wahiduddin: We bestowed Isaac and then Jacob on him as an additional boon and We made all of them righteous
t.shakir: And We gave him Ishaq and Yaqoub, a son's son, and We made (them) all good
t.haleem: and We gave him Isaac and Jacob as an additional gift, and made each of them righteous
t.maariful: And We blessed him with IsHāq and Ya‘qūb as gift, and each one of them We made righteous
t.khattab: And We blessed him with Isaac ˹as a son˺ and Jacob ˹as a grandson˺, as an additional favour—making all of them righteous
t.ghali: And We bestowed upon him Ibrahim and Yacuq?b, (Isaac and Jacob) (additional) gift (to him), and each (one) of them We made righteous
t.irving: We bestowed Isaac and (then) Jacob on him as an additional boon; each We made into honorable men
t.iwq: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in addition, and all of them We made righteous

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami telah memberikan kepada-nya (Ibrahim) lshak dan Ya'qub, sebagai suatu anugerah (daripada Kami). Dan masing-masingnya Kami jadikan orang-orang yang saleh
t_id.sabiq: Lalu Ibrâhîm Kami karuniakan Ishâq, dan Ya'qûb setelah Ishâq, sebagai tambahan atas permintaannya. Baik Ishâq maupun Ya'qûb, keduanya kemudian Kami jadikan orang-orang yang saleh.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami telah memberikan kepadanya) kepada Ibrahim, yang sebelumnya selalu mendambakan mempunyai seorang anak, sebagaimana yang disebutkan di dalam surah Ash-Shaffat (Ishak dan Yakub sebagai suatu anugerah) dari Kami, yaitu anugerah yang lebih daripada apa yang dimintanya. Atau yang dimaksud dengan Naafilah adalah cucu (Dan masing-masingnya) Nabi Ibrahim dan kedua anaknya itu (Kami jadikan orang-orang yang saleh) yakni menjadi nabi semuanya.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami telah memberikan kepada-nya (Ibrahim) lshak dan Ya'qub, sebagai suatu anugerah (daripada Kami). Dan masing-masingnya Kami jadikan orang-orang yang saleh

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ووهبنا له» أي لإبراهيم وكان سأل ولداً كما ذكر في الصافات «إسحاق ويعقوب نافلة» أي زيادة على المسؤول أو هو ولد الولد «وكلاً» أي هو وولداه «جعلنا صالحين» أنبياء
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسۡحَٰقَ وَيَعۡقُوبَ نَافِلَةٗۖ وَكُلّٗا جَعَلۡنَا صَٰلِحِينَ (نَافِلَةً: زِيَادَةً عَمَّا سَأَلَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami telah memberikan kepadanya) kepada Ibrahim, yang sebelumnya selalu mendambakan mempunyai seorang anak, sebagaimana yang disebutkan di dalam surah Ash-Shaffat (Ishak dan Yakub sebagai suatu anugerah) dari Kami, yaitu anugerah yang lebih daripada apa yang dimintanya. Atau yang dimaksud dengan Naafilah adalah cucu (Dan masing-masingnya) Nabi Ibrahim dan kedua anaknya itu (Kami jadikan orang-orang yang saleh) yakni menjadi nabi semuanya

ar: وَجَعَلۡنَـٰهُمۡ أَئِمَّةࣰ یَهۡدُونَ بِأَمۡرِنَا وَأَوۡحَیۡنَاۤ إِلَیۡهِمۡ فِعۡلَ ٱلۡخَیۡرَ ٰتِ وَإِقَامَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَإِیتَاۤءَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةِۖ وَكَانُوا۟ لَنَا عَـٰبِدِینَ
ar_simple: وَجَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَهْدُونَ بِأَمْرِنَا وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ فِعْلَ الْخَيْرَاتِ وَإِقَامَ الصَّلَاةِ وَإِيتَاءَ الزَّكَاةِ ۖ وَكَانُوا لَنَا عَابِدِينَ

t.sahih-int: And We made them leaders guiding by Our command. And We inspired to them the doing of good deeds, establishment of prayer, and giving of zakah; and they were worshippers of Us
t.asad: and made them leaders who would guide [others] in accordance with Our behest: for We inspired them [with a will] to do good works, and to be constant in prayer, and to dispense charity: and Us [alone] did they worship
t.yusuf-ali: And We made them leaders, guiding (men) by Our Command, and We sent them inspiration to do good deeds, to establish regular prayers, and to practise regular charity; and they constantly served Us (and Us only)
t.pickthall: And We made them chiefs who guide by Our command, and We inspired in them the doing of good deeds and the right establishment of worship and the giving of alms, and they were worshippers of Us (alone)
t.arberry: and appointed them to be leaders guiding by Our command, and We revealed to them the doing of good deeds, and to perform the prayer, and to pay the alms, and Us they served
t.hilali-khan: And We made them leaders, guiding (mankind) by Our Command, and We inspired in them the doing of good deeds, performing Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), and the giving of Zakat and of Us (Alone) they were worshippers
t.itani: And We made them leaders, guiding by Our command; and We inspired them to do good works, and to observe the prayer, and to give out charity. They were devoted servants to Us.
t.mubarakpuri: And We made them leaders, guiding by Our command, and We revealed to them the doing of good deeds, performing Salah, and the giving of Zakah, and of Us (Alone) they were the worshippers.
t.qarib: We made them leaders guiding others by Our command. And We inspired them to do good deeds, to establish prayer, and to give the purifying alms; they were our true worshippers
t.wahiduddin: We made them leaders who guided people by Our command. We revealed to them the doing of good, observance of prayer and the giving of alms and Us alone did they worship
t.shakir: And We made them Imams who guided (people) by Our command, and We revealed to them the doing of good and the keeping up of prayer and the giving of the alms, and Us (alone) did they serve
t.haleem: We made all of them leaders, guiding others by Our command, and We inspired them to do good works, to keep up the prayer, and to give alms: they were Our true worshippers
t.maariful: And We made them the Imams who guided (people) under Our command, and We inspired them to do good deeds and to establish Salāh and pay Zakāh, and Us alone they worshipped
t.khattab: We ˹also˺ made them leaders, guiding by Our command, and inspired them to do good deeds, establish prayer, and pay alms-tax. And they were devoted to Our worship
t.ghali: And We made them Imams (i.e., leaders) guiding by Our Command, and We revealed to them the performance of charitable deeds, and keeping up the prayer, and the bringing of the Zakat; (i.e., payment of poor-dues) and they were worshipers of Us (alone)
t.irving: We made them leaders who guided (others) by Our command and We inspired them to perform good deeds, keep up prayer, and pay the welfare tax. They have been serving Us
t.iwq: And We made them leaders guiding by Our command. And We inspired to them the doing of good deeds, establishment of prayer, and giving of zakah; and they were worshippers of Us

t_id.kemenag: Kami telah menjadikan mereka itu sebagai pemimpin-pemimpin yang memberi petunjuk dengan perintah Kami dan telah Kami wahyukan kepada, mereka mengerjakan kebajikan, mendirikan sembahyang, menunaikan zakat, dan hanya kepada Kamilah mereka selalu menyembah,
t_id.sabiq: Mereka juga Kami jadikan sebagai nabi yang menyeru dan menunjuki manusia kepada kebaikan dengan perintah Kami. Hal itu setelah Kami mengilhami mereka untuk melakukan kebaikan, mengerjakan salat dengan betul dan membayar zakat. Mereka pun kemudian tunduk dan ikhlas kepada Kami.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kami telah menjadikan mereka itu sebagian pemimpin-pemimpin) dapat dibaca A-immatan atau Ayimmatan, yakni pemimpin yang menjadi teladan dalam kebaikan (yang memberi petunjuk) kepada manusia (dengan perintah Kami) memberi petunjuk kepada mereka untuk memeluk agama Kami (dan telah Kami wahyukan kepada mereka mengerjakan kebajikan, mendirikan salat, menunaikan zakat) hendaknya mereka dan orang-orang yang mengikuti mereka mengerjakan semuanya itu. Huruf Ha dari lafal Iqaamah dibuang demi untuk meringankan bunyi, sehingga menjadi Iqaamash Shalaati bukan Iqaamatish Shalaati (dan hanya kepada Kamilah mereka selalu menyembah).
t_id.iwq: Kami telah menjadikan mereka itu sebagai pemimpin-pemimpin yang memberi petunjuk dengan perintah Kami dan telah Kami wahyukan kepada, mereka mengerjakan kebajikan, mendirikan sembahyang, menunaikan zakat, dan hanya kepada Kamilah mereka selalu menyembah,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وجعلناهم أئمة» بتحقيق الهمزتين وإبدال الثانية ياء يقتدى بهم في الخير «يهدون» الناس «بأمرنا» إلى ديننا «وأوحينا إليهم فعل الخيرات وإقام الصلاة وإيتاء الزكاة» أي أن تفعل وتقام وتؤتى منهم ومن أتباعهم، وحذف هاء إقامة تخفيف «وكانوا لنا عابدين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ أَئِمَّةٗ يَهۡدُونَ بِأَمۡرِنَا وَأَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَيۡهِمۡ فِعۡلَ ٱلۡخَيۡرَٰتِ وَإِقَامَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَإِيتَآءَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةِۖ وَكَانُواْ لَنَا عَٰبِدِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kami telah menjadikan mereka itu sebagian pemimpin-pemimpin) dapat dibaca A-immatan atau Ayimmatan, yakni pemimpin yang menjadi teladan dalam kebaikan (yang memberi petunjuk) kepada manusia (dengan perintah Kami) memberi petunjuk kepada mereka untuk memeluk agama Kami (dan telah Kami wahyukan kepada mereka mengerjakan kebajikan, mendirikan salat, menunaikan zakat) hendaknya mereka dan orang-orang yang mengikuti mereka mengerjakan semuanya itu. Huruf Ha dari lafal Iqaamah dibuang demi untuk meringankan bunyi, sehingga menjadi Iqaamash Shalaati bukan Iqaamatish Shalaati (dan hanya kepada Kamilah mereka selalu menyembah)

ar: وَلُوطًا ءَاتَیۡنَـٰهُ حُكۡمࣰا وَعِلۡمࣰا وَنَجَّیۡنَـٰهُ مِنَ ٱلۡقَرۡیَةِ ٱلَّتِی كَانَت تَّعۡمَلُ ٱلۡخَبَـٰۤئِثَۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُوا۟ قَوۡمَ سَوۡءࣲ فَـٰسِقِینَ
ar_simple: وَلُوطًا آتَيْنَاهُ حُكْمًا وَعِلْمًا وَنَجَّيْنَاهُ مِنَ الْقَرْيَةِ الَّتِي كَانَتْ تَعْمَلُ الْخَبَائِثَ ۗ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا قَوْمَ سَوْءٍ فَاسِقِينَ

t.sahih-int: And to Lot We gave judgement and knowledge, and We saved him from the city that was committing wicked deeds. Indeed, they were a people of evil, defiantly disobedient
t.asad: AND UNTO Lot, too, We vouchsafed sound judgment and knowledge [of right and wrong], and saved him from that community which was given to deeds of abomination. [We destroyed those people - forr,] verily, they were people lost in evil, depraved –
t.yusuf-ali: And to Lut, too, We gave Judgment and Knowledge, and We saved him from the town which practised abominations: truly they were a people given to Evil, a rebellious people
t.pickthall: And unto Lot we gave judgment and knowledge, and We delivered him from the community that did abominations. Lo! they were folk of evil, lewd
t.arberry: And Lot -- to him We gave judgment and knowledge; and we delivered him from the city that had been doing deeds of corruption; they were an evil people, truly ungodly
t.hilali-khan: And (remember) Lout (Lot), We gave him Hukman (right judgement of the affairs and Prophethood) and (religious) knowledge, and We saved him from the town (folk) who practised Al-Khaba'ith (evil, wicked and filthy deeds, etc.). Verily, they were a people given to evil, and were Fasiqun (rebellious, disobedient, to Allah)
t.itani: And Lot—We gave him judgment and knowledge, and We delivered him from the town that practiced the abominations. They were wicked and perverted people.
t.mubarakpuri: And (remember) Lut, We gave him wisdom and knowledge, and We saved him from the town who practised Al-Khaba'ith. Verily, they were a people given to evil, and were rebellious.
t.qarib: And to Lot We gave sound judgment and knowledge, and We saved him from the town that was committing wicked deeds. They were evil, perverse people
t.wahiduddin: To Lot We gave wisdom and knowledge and delivered him from the city which practiced abomination. They were indeed a wicked people
t.shakir: And (as for) Lut, We gave him wisdom and knowledge, and We delivered him from the town which wrought abominations; surely they were an evil people, transgressors
t.haleem: We gave Lot sound judgement and knowledge and saved him from the community who practised obscenities- they were shameless people who broke God’s law
t.maariful: As for LūT, We gave him wisdom and knowledge, and We delivered him from the town that used to do dirty deeds. Indeed, they were the people of evil, the sinners
t.khattab: And to Lot We gave wisdom and knowledge, and delivered him from the society engrossed in shameful practices. They were certainly an evil, rebellious people
t.ghali: And Lut, (Lot) to him We brought judgment and knowledge, and We safely delivered him from the town that had been doing wicked deeds. Surely they were a woeful people, (and) immoral
t.irving: And Lot We gave discretion and knowledge, and saved him from the town which had been performing wicked deeds. They had been such evil folk, perverts
t.iwq: And to Lot We gave judgement and knowledge, and We saved him from the city that was committing wicked deeds. Indeed, they were a people of evil, defiantly disobedient

t_id.kemenag: dan kepada Luth, Kami telah berikan hikmah dan ilmu, dan telah Kami selamatkan dia dari (azab yang telah menimpa penduduk) kota yang mengerjakan perbuatan keji. Sesungguhnya mereka adalah kaum yang jahat lagi fasik,
