t.asad: But if they turn away, say: “I have proclaimed this in equity unto all of you alike; but I do not know whether that [judgment] which you are promised [by God] is near or far [in time]
t.yusuf-ali: But if they turn back, Say: "I have proclaimed the Message to you all alike and in truth; but I know not whether that which ye are promised is near or far
t.pickthall: But if they are averse, then say: I have warned you all alike, although I know not whether nigh or far is that which ye are promised
t.arberry: Then, if they should turn their backs, say: 'I have proclaimed to you all equally, even though I know not whether near or far is that you are promised
t.hilali-khan: But if they (disbelievers, idolaters, Jews, Christians, polytheists, etc.) turn away (from Islamic Monotheism) say (to them O Muhammad SAW): "I give you a notice (of war as) to be known to us all alike. And I know not whether that which you are promised (i.e. the torment or the Day of Resurrection) is near or far
t.itani: But if they turn away, say, “I have informed you sufficiently. Although I do not know whether what you are promised is near or far.”
t.mubarakpuri: But if they turn away say: "I give you a notice to be known to us all alike. And I know not whether that which you are promised is near or far."
t.qarib: But, if they turn away, then say, "I have proclaimed the message equally to [all of] you. And I do not know if what you have been promised is near or far
t.wahiduddin: If they turn away, say, "I have warned you all alike, though I do not know whether [the scourge] which you are promised is near at hand or far off
t.shakir: But if they turn back, say: I have given you warning in fairness and I do not know whether what you are threatened with is near or far
t.haleem: But if they turn away, say, ‘I have proclaimed the message fairly to you all. I do not know whether the judgement you are promised is near or far
t.maariful: But, if they turn away, then say (to them), “I have warned you all alike, and I do not know whether what you have been warned of is near or far
t.khattab: If they turn away, then say, “I have warned you all equally. I do not know if what you are threatened with is near or far
t.ghali: So if they turn away, then say, "I have notified you (all) equally, and decidedly I do not realize whether what you are promised is near or far
t.irving: If they should turn away, then SAY: "I have announced it to you all alike. If I only knew whether what you are promised is near or far away
t.iwq: But if they turn away, then say, "I have announced to all of you equally. And I know not whether near or far is that which you are promised

t_id.kemenag: Jika mereka berpaling, maka katakanlah: "Aku telah menyampaikan kepada kamu sekalian (ajaran) yang sama (antara kita) dan aku tidak mengetahui apakah yang diancamkan kepadamu itu sudah dekat atau masih jauh?".
t_id.sabiq: Jika mereka perpaling dari ajakanmu, katakanlah kepada mereka, "Aku telah memberitahu kalian semua hal yang telah diperintahkan Allah kepadaku. Dengan demikian, pengetahuan kita semua menjadi sama. Aku tidak tahu apakah janji tentang pembangkitan dan pembalasan itu telah dekat atau masih jauh.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Jika mereka berpaling) dari hal tersebut (maka katakanlah, "Aku telah menyerukan kepada kalian) maksudnya telah memberitahukan kepada kalian untuk berperang (dalam keadaan sama) lafal 'Alaa Sawaa-in ini menjadi Hal dari Fa'il dan Maf'ul. Maksudnya, antara kami dan kalian sama-sama mengetahui maklumat ini, aku tidak memaksakan kalian untuk berperang, tetapi kalian sendirilah yang mengajaknya, maka bersiap-siaplah kalian (dan tidaklah) yakni tiadalah (aku mengetahui, apakah yang diancamkan kepada kalian itu sudah dekat atau masih jauh") maksudnya azab atau kiamat yang juga di dalamnya mengandung azab, akan tetapi sesungguhnya yang mengetahui hal itu hanyalah Allah semata.
t_id.iwq: Jika mereka berpaling, maka katakanlah: "Aku telah menyampaikan kepada kamu sekalian (ajaran) yang sama (antara kita) dan aku tidak mengetahui apakah yang diancamkan kepadamu itu sudah dekat atau masih jauh?".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فإن تولوا» عن ذلك «فقل آذنتكم» أعلمتكم بالحرب «على سواء» حال من الفاعل والمفعول، أي مستوين في علمه لا أستبد به دونكم لتتأهبوا «وإن» ما «أدري أقريب أم بعيد ما توعدون» من العذاب أو القيامة المشتملة عليه وإنما يعلمه الله
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَإِن تَوَلَّوۡاْ فَقُلۡ ءَاذَنتُكُمۡ عَلَىٰ سَوَآءٖۖ وَإِنۡ أَدۡرِيٓ أَقَرِيبٌ أَم بَعِيدٞ مَّا تُوعَدُونَ (آذَنتُكُمْ: أَعْلَمْتُكُمْ مَا أُمِرْتُ بِهِ, عَلَى سَوَاءٍ: أَنَا وَأَنْتُمْ مُسْتَوُونَ فِي العِلْمِ بِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Jika mereka berpaling) dari hal tersebut (maka katakanlah, "Aku telah menyerukan kepada kalian) maksudnya telah memberitahukan kepada kalian untuk berperang (dalam keadaan sama) lafal 'Alaa Sawaa-in ini menjadi Hal dari Fa'il dan Maf'ul. Maksudnya, antara kami dan kalian sama-sama mengetahui maklumat ini, aku tidak memaksakan kalian untuk berperang, tetapi kalian sendirilah yang mengajaknya, maka bersiap-siaplah kalian (dan tidaklah) yakni tiadalah (aku mengetahui, apakah yang diancamkan kepada kalian itu sudah dekat atau masih jauh") maksudnya azab atau kiamat yang juga di dalamnya mengandung azab, akan tetapi sesungguhnya yang mengetahui hal itu hanyalah Allah semata

ar: إِنَّهُۥ یَعۡلَمُ ٱلۡجَهۡرَ مِنَ ٱلۡقَوۡلِ وَیَعۡلَمُ مَا تَكۡتُمُونَ
ar_simple: إِنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْجَهْرَ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تَكْتُمُونَ

t.sahih-int: Indeed, He knows what is declared of speech, and He knows what you conceal
t.asad: “Verily, He knows all that is said openly, just as He [alone] knows all that you would conceal
t.yusuf-ali: It is He Who knows what is open in speech and what ye hide (in your hearts)
t.pickthall: Lo! He knoweth that which is said openly, and that which ye conceal
t.arberry: Surely He knows what is spoken aloud and He knows what you hide
t.hilali-khan: (Say O Muhammad SAW) Verily, He (Allah) knows that which is spoken aloud (openly) and that which you conceal
t.itani: He knows what is said openly, and He knows what you conceal.
t.mubarakpuri: "Verily, He knows that which is spoken aloud and He knows that which you conceal."
t.qarib: He knows what is revealed when you speak, and He knows what you conceal
t.wahiduddin: God surely knows what you say openly and also knows what you conceal
t.shakir: Surely He knows what is spoken openly and He knows what you hide
t.haleem: but He knows what you reveal and conceal
t.maariful: Surely, He knows what is spoken openly and He knows what you conceal
t.khattab: Allah surely knows what you say openly and whatever you hide
t.ghali: Surely He knows what is spoken aloud, (literally: loud saying) and He knows what you keep back
t.irving: He knows anything one says out loud and He (also) knows whatever you conceal
t.iwq: Indeed, He knows what is declared of speech, and He knows what you conceal

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Dia mengetahui perkataan (yang kamu ucapkan) dengan terang-terangan dan Dia mengetahui apa yang kamu rahasiakan.
t_id.sabiq: Allah benar-benar mengetahui semua hal yang terucap, baik secara terang-terangan maupun dalam hati.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Dia) Allah swt. (mengetahui perkataan yang terang-terangan) dan juga perbuatan kalian dan orang-orang selain kalian (dan Dia mengetahui apa yang kalian rahasiakan) yang dirahasiakan kalian dan selain kalian.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Dia mengetahui perkataan (yang kamu ucapkan) dengan terang-terangan dan Dia mengetahui apa yang kamu rahasiakan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنه» تعالى «يعلم الجهر من القول» والفعل منكم ومن غيركم «ويعلم ما تكتمون» أنتم وغيركم من السر
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّهُۥ يَعۡلَمُ ٱلۡجَهۡرَ مِنَ ٱلۡقَوۡلِ وَيَعۡلَمُ مَا تَكۡتُمُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Dia) Allah swt. (mengetahui perkataan yang terang-terangan) dan juga perbuatan kalian dan orang-orang selain kalian (dan Dia mengetahui apa yang kalian rahasiakan) yang dirahasiakan kalian dan selain kalian

ar: وَإِنۡ أَدۡرِی لَعَلَّهُۥ فِتۡنَةࣱ لَّكُمۡ وَمَتَـٰعٌ إِلَىٰ حِینࣲ
ar_simple: وَإِنْ أَدْرِي لَعَلَّهُ فِتْنَةٌ لَكُمْ وَمَتَاعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ

t.sahih-int: And I know not; perhaps it is a trial for you and enjoyment for a time
t.asad: But [as for me,] I do not know whether, perchance, this [delay in God’s judgment] is but a trial for you, and a [merciful] respite for a while.”
t.yusuf-ali: I know not but that it may be a trial for you, and a grant of (worldly) livelihood (to you) for a time
t.pickthall: And I know not but that this may be a trial for you, and enjoyment for a while
t.arberry: I know not; haply it is a trial for you and an enjoyment for a time
t.hilali-khan: And I know not, perhaps it may be a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a while
t.itani: “And I do not know whether it is perhaps a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a while.”
t.mubarakpuri: "And I know not, perhaps it may be a trial for you, and an enjoyment for a while."
t.qarib: For all I know [this delay in God's judgment] may be a trial for you and you will have enjoyment for a while
t.wahiduddin: Nor do I know whether it may mean a trial for you and a short reprieve
t.shakir: And I do not know if this may be a trial for you and a provision till a time
t.haleem: I do not know: this [time] may well be a test for you, and enjoyment for a while.’
t.maariful: And I do not know; perhaps it is a trial for you and an enjoyment for a while.”
t.khattab: I do not know if this ˹delay˺ is possibly a test for you and an enjoyment for a while.”
t.ghali: And decidedly I do not realize (that) perhaps it would be a temptation for you, and an enjoyment for a while." (i.e., your disbelief)
t.irving: If I only knew whether it may mean a trial for you as well as enjoyment for a while
t.iwq: And I know not; perhaps it is a trial for you and enjoyment for a time

t_id.kemenag: Dan aku tiada mengetahui, boleh jadi hal itu cobaan bagi kamu dan kesenangan sampai kepada suatu waktu.
t_id.sabiq: Aku juga tidak tahu, barangkali penundaan azab itu adalah ujian dari Allah. Dia sengaja membiarkan kalian menikmati kesenangan hidup sampai suatu waktu yang telah ditentukan Allah menurut kebijaksanaan-Nya."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidaklah) (aku mengetahui, barangkali hal itu) apa yang telah aku beritahukan kepada kalian dan belum diketahui saatnya (sebagai cobaan) ujian (bagi kalian) supaya dapat dilihat, apakah yang diperbuat oleh kalian dan kesenangan) yakni bersenang-senang (sampai kepada suatu waktu) maksudnya sampai habisnya umur kalian. Pengertian ayat ini hanya bersifat kebalikan daripada hal yang diharapkan dengan memakai ungkapan La'alla, akan tetapi bukan subjek daripada harapan tersebut.
t_id.iwq: Dan aku tiada mengetahui, boleh jadi hal itu cobaan bagi kamu dan kesenangan sampai kepada suatu waktu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإن» ما «أدري لعله» أي ما أعلمتكم به ولم يعلم وقته «فتنة» اختبار «لكم» ليرى كيف صنعكم «ومتاع» تمتع «إلى حين» أي انقضاء آجالكم وهذا مقابل للأول المترجى بلعل وليس الثاني محلا للترجي
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِنۡ أَدۡرِي لَعَلَّهُۥ فِتۡنَةٞ لَّكُمۡ وَمَتَٰعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٖ (وَإِنْ أَدْرِي: لَسْتُ أَدْرِي, لَعَلَّهُ فِتْنَةٌ: لَعَلَّ تَاخِيرَ العَذَابِ الَّذِي اسْتَعْجَلْتُمُوهُ اسْتِدْرَاجٌ لَكُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidaklah) (aku mengetahui, barangkali hal itu) apa yang telah aku beritahukan kepada kalian dan belum diketahui saatnya (sebagai cobaan) ujian (bagi kalian) supaya dapat dilihat, apakah yang diperbuat oleh kalian dan kesenangan) yakni bersenang-senang (sampai kepada suatu waktu) maksudnya sampai habisnya umur kalian. Pengertian ayat ini hanya bersifat kebalikan daripada hal yang diharapkan dengan memakai ungkapan La'alla, akan tetapi bukan subjek daripada harapan tersebut

ar: قَـٰلَ رَبِّ ٱحۡكُم بِٱلۡحَقِّۗ وَرَبُّنَا ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنُ ٱلۡمُسۡتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ
ar_simple: قَالَ رَبِّ احْكُمْ بِالْحَقِّ ۗ وَرَبُّنَا الرَّحْمَٰنُ الْمُسْتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ

t.sahih-int: [The Prophet] has said, "My Lord, judge [between us] in truth. And our Lord is the Most Merciful, the one whose help is sought against that which you describe
t.asad: Say: “O my Sustainer! Judge Thou in truth!” - and [say]: “Our Sustainer is the Most Gracious, the One whose aid is ever to be sought against all your [attempts at] defining [Him]!”
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "O my Lord! judge Thou in truth!" "Our Lord Most Gracious is the One Whose assistance should be sought against the blasphemies ye utter
t.pickthall: He saith: My Lord! Judge Thou with truth. Our Lord is the Beneficent, Whose help is to be implored against that which ye ascribe (unto Him)
t.arberry: He said: 'My Lord, judge Thou with truth! And our Lord is the All-merciful; His succour is ever to be sought against that you describe
t.hilali-khan: He (Muhammad SAW) said:"My Lord! Judge You in truth! Our Lord is the Most Beneficent, Whose Help is to be sought against that which you attribute (unto Allah that He has offspring, and unto Muhammad SAW that he is a sorcerer, and unto the Quran that it is poetry, etc)
t.itani: He said, “My Lord, judge with justice.” And, “Our Lord is the Gracious, Whose help is sought against what you allege.”
t.mubarakpuri: He said:"My Lord! Judge You in truth! Our Lord is the Most Gracious, Whose help is to be sought against that which you attribute!"
t.qarib: Say [Prophet] "My Lord, judge [between us] in Truth. Our Lord is the Merciful-to-all, the one whose help is ever sought against that which you unbelievers speak
t.wahiduddin: Say, "My Lord, judge with truth. Our Lord is the Gracious One whose help we seek against what you utter
t.shakir: He said: O my Lord! judge Thou with truth; and our Lord is the Beneficent Allah, Whose help is sought against what you ascribe (to Him)
t.haleem: He said,‘My Lord, pass the true judgement.’ And, ‘Our Lord is the Lord of Mercy. We seek His assistance against what you [disbelievers] say.’
t.maariful: He (Our Messenger) said, “My Lord, judge with truth. And Our Lord is the RaHmān (All-Merciful), the One whose help is sought against what you describe
t.khattab: ˹In the end,˺ the Prophet said, “My Lord! Judge ˹between us˺ in truth. And our Lord is the Most Compassionate, Whose help is sought against what you claim.”
t.ghali: He said, "Lord! Judge with the truth! And our Lord is The All-Merciful; His help is to be sought against whatever you describe
t.irving: He said: "My Lord, judge with Truth," and "Our Lord is the Mercygiving Whose help is sought against what you describe
t.iwq: The Prophet has said, "My Lord, judge between us in truth. And our Lord is the Ever-Merciful, the one whose help is sought against that which you describe

t_id.kemenag: (Muhammad) berkata: "Ya Tuhanku, berilah keputusan dengan adil. Dan Tuhan kami ialah Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah lagi Yang dimohonkan pertolongan-Nya terhadap apa yang kamu katakan".
t_id.sabiq: Katakanlah, wahai Nabi, "Tuhanku, hukumlah aku dan orang-orang yang telah aku sampaikan wahyu-Mu secara adil! Suatu pengadilan yang membuat tidak sama antara orang-orang Mukmin dengan orang-orang kafir. Tuhan kami adalah Pemberi nikmat yang mulia, berhak dipuji dan disyukuri, serta tempat meminta pertolongan dalam menyangkal kebohongan-kebohongan yang kalian lakukan terhadap Allah, wahai orang-orang kafir."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Muhammad berkata) qaala menurut suatu qiraat dibaca Qul, yakni katakanlah hai Muhammad, ("Ya Rabbku! Berilah keputusan) antara aku dan orang-orang yang mendustakan aku (dengan adil) yakni azab bagi mereka atau pertolongan-Mu di dalam menghadapi mereka. Maka akhirnya mereka diazab di dalam perang Badar, Uhud, Hunain, Ahzab dan Khandaq, Nabi saw. mendapat kemenangan atas mereka. (Dan Rabb kami ialah Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah lagi Yang dimohonkan pertolongan-Nya terhadap apa yang kalian katakan itu") kedustaan perkataan kalian kepada Allah: kalian telah mengatakan-Nya, bahwa Allah mempunyai anak. Perkataan kalian kepadaku, bahwa aku ini adalah seorang penyihir. Perkataan kalian terhadap Alquran, bahwa ia adalah syair semata.
t_id.iwq: (Muhammad) berkata: "Ya Tuhanku, berilah keputusan dengan adil. Dan Tuhan kami ialah Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah lagi Yang dimohonkan pertolongan-Nya terhadap apa yang kamu katakan".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (قل) وفي قراءة قال (رب احكم) بيني وبين مكذبي (بالحق) بالعذاب لهم أو النصر عليهم، فعذبوا ببدر وأحد وحنين والأحزاب والخندق ونصر عليهم (وربنا الرحمن المستعان على ما تصفون) من كذبكم على الله في قولكم "" اتخذ ولداً "" وعلي في قولكم: ساحر، وعلى القرآن في قولكم: شعر
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَٰلَ رَبِّ ٱحۡكُم بِٱلۡحَقِّۗ وَرَبُّنَا ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ ٱلۡمُسۡتَعَانُ عَلَىٰ مَا تَصِفُونَ (احْكُم بِالْحَقِّ: اِفْصِلْ بَيْنَنَا، وَبَيْنَ المُكَذِّبِينَ بِالقَضَاءِ الحَقِّ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Muhammad berkata) qaala menurut suatu qiraat dibaca Qul, yakni katakanlah hai Muhammad, ("Ya Rabbku! Berilah keputusan) antara aku dan orang-orang yang mendustakan aku (dengan adil) yakni azab bagi mereka atau pertolongan-Mu di dalam menghadapi mereka. Maka akhirnya mereka diazab di dalam perang Badar, Uhud, Hunain, Ahzab dan Khandaq, Nabi saw. mendapat kemenangan atas mereka. (Dan Rabb kami ialah Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah lagi Yang dimohonkan pertolongan-Nya terhadap apa yang kalian katakan itu") kedustaan perkataan kalian kepada Allah: kalian telah mengatakan-Nya, bahwa Allah mempunyai anak. Perkataan kalian kepadaku, bahwa aku ini adalah seorang penyihir. Perkataan kalian terhadap Alquran, bahwa ia adalah syair semata
